info.name: MD Integrations API
info.description: ## Intro The MD Integrations API is a set of HTTP endpoints that help your app integrate with MD Integrations. The API is integrated with [DoseSpot](https://www.dosespot.com/), an e-Prescribing system. Our API acts as proxy to DoseSpot: all the required actions on DoseSpot side are handled under the hood by our API. [](https://drive.google.com/file/d/13CSUQsgAbcIETjONdjBhCrjCzitDdRfe/view?usp=sharing) **##** Base URL - [https://api.mdintegrations.com](https://api.mdintegrations.com) ## Request and Response Formats All API access is over HTTPS. All data is sent and received as JSON. ## Headers Please note that all requests require at least these two headers: Content-Type: application/json Accept: application/json ## API Errors #### Error Codes The following table describes all the possible HTTP error codes. | Code | Description | | --- | --- | | 400 | Bad request | | 401 | Unauthorized. This can happen if the access token is invalid, expired or has been revoked | | 403 | The access token doesn't have access to perform the requested action | | 404 | Resource not found | | 418 | API under maintenance | | 5XX | An error occurred on the MD Integrations servers. Check MD Integrations Status API for more details or contact the support team | #### Error Format The following table describes the top-level JSON object attributes present in the body of error messages. | key | value | | --- | --- | | error | A detailed error message \* _on production this field is not returned_ | | message | Friendly error message. This error message can be used to be displayed to the final user on the app | ## Authentication To make any request to the MD Integrations API for Partners, you must first generate a brand new `access token` using your **Client ID** and **Client Secret**. Both the **Client ID** and **Client Secret** should be provided to you by the MD Integrations Team. > ⚠️ Be careful with the Client Secret! It should not be shared and should be kept safe. ## Issuing an access token The expected flow is shown in the image below: [Image source](https://developer.ebay.com/api-docs/static/oauth-client-credentials-grant.html) As our authorization API you should use the **Partners > Request access token** endpoint - see it's description for more details. After you've created a new access token, you should be ready to make authenticated requests using the HTTP `Authorization` header with the value `Bearer` . E.g.: ``` http GET /partner HTTP/1.1 ... Authorization: Bearer eyJ0eXAiOiJKV1Q... ``` ## Expired tokens Whenever your token gets expired, you should create a brand new token using the same flow described above. ## Terminology - **patient**: person in need of treatment by a physician; - **clinician**: a qualified practitioner of medicine. Alias to MD, doctor and physician; - **partner**: a partner is a company who sells medications and compounds directly to its patients; - **medication**: a substance used for medical treatment, especially a medicine or drug, regularly made by pharmaceutical companies; - **compound**: drug that is specifically mixed and prepared for an individual patient, meaning they are not readily available like a regular OTC medication; - **prescription**: an instruction written by a clinician that authorizes a patient to be provided by a medication; - **case**: set of medication prescriptions requests that the partner sends to MD-Integrations on behalf of a patient. ## Contact Support - Email: [support@mdintegrations.com](https://mailto:support@mdintegrations.com)

request.description:
Update cases files.

| field | type | description |
|-------|:----:|------------:|
| case_files | array(uuid)* | Files ID to be attached to the case. The files must be uploaded before creating a case. To upload files check **Partners > Files** |

request.description:
Create a case question.

# Payload

| field | type | description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| question | text\* | Question |
| answer | text\* | Answer |
| type | string\* | Question type. Available options: `boolean`, `number` or `string` |
| important | boolean | Flag to set a question as important |
| display_in_pdf | boolean | Flag to set if this question will be displayed in the case PDF file |
| description | text | A description for your question |
| label | string(50) | The question label |
| metadata | text | A metadata field |
| displayed_options | array | An array of options that were displayed to the patient |
| is_critical | boolean | Mark question as critical |

                        "clinical_note": "PMH: [patient_name] is a [patient_age] year old [patient_gender] located in [patient_state] who presents via [partner_name] for a virtual interactive telemedicine evaluation as above.\n\nPt listed current medications taken as: [patient_reported_medications]\n\nEXAM: I have performed my clinical evaluation through review of the patient’s current symptoms, past medical history, clinical photos, allergies and all other chart information.\n\nDIAGNOSIS: [icd_10]\n\nTREATMENT: I have prescribed treatment with [prescriptions]\n\nPatient has been educated on the treatment plan including diagnosis, instructions for use, risks/benefits/side effects/alternatives to therapy. If any side effects occur they should discontinue use and contact me for further instruction, except in the case of severe side effects in which case they should seek urgent care in an emergency room.\n\nFOLLOW UP: The patient has been provided information on how to contact me directly to follow up if any medical concerns or reactions to treatment arise at any time. The patient is recommended to follow up with myself or the support team if additional concerns arise or the treatment plan needs to be adjusted. I am and will remain immediately available through chat, phone, and video communication throughout the treatment course. Patient encouraged to notify PCP of today's telemedicine visit/treatment plan, and to continue to follow up with routine visits for their general health including annual exams. Patient provided information on how to find a local PCP if they do not already have one. Patient has been educated that there are limitations to telemedicine care, and in-person care may be necessary in certain situations (ER if severe side effects develop or through an in person physician if signs/symptoms develop that warrant additional work up/evaluation/alternative treatments).\nTo the extent that I have been made aware by the company with which I contract to provide medical services, the patient has signed relevant attestations prior to being assigned to me as a clinician that state that the patient is aware of any limitations in a virtual health encounter, and understands that urgent or emergent care must be pursued in person). I have confirmed the patient's identity via review of a legally recognized form of ID or through Vouched (a second party identity verification service). The patient is over 18 and able to consent for their own care.\nPlease see doctor/patient messaging thread for any relevant dialogue which, in addition to providing supplemental medical information as needed, secures and meets the legally-defined doctor-patient relationship. Get the following case details:

- Patient
- Partner
- Priority status and reason
- Status (if is cancelled or not)

To get details from `prescriptions`, `files` and `questions` you must use specific API endpoints:

- Files: check **"Partners > Cases > Files"**
- Prescriptions: check **"Partners > Cases > Prescriptions"**
- Questions: check **"Partners > Cases > Questions"**

request.description:
Return a list of cases according to a given status.

The following statuses are available:

- **Created**: a list of cases create and not assigned yet;
    
- **Assigned**: a list of cases assigned to the clinician waiting for review;
    
- **Waiting**: a list of cases assigned to the clinician, waiting for the patient information or review;
    
- **Cancelled**: a list of cases assigned to the clinician and canceled by the partner;
    
- **Support**: a list of cases assigned to the clinician and transferred to support;
    
- **Processing**: a list of cases assigned to the clinician that were already reviewed and finished by the clinician, but with one or more prescriptions waiting for DoseSpot confirmation;
    
- **Completed**: a list of cases assigned to the clinician with all its prescriptions already confirmed by DoseSpot (prescriptions already verified by the pharmacies).
    

## Payload

| field | type | description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| partners | array(uuid) | If passed, all cases will belong to this partner. |
| clinicians | array(uuid) | If passed, all cases assigned to this clinician will be displayed. |
| sort | string | Sorts by created_at from the Case Status, desc or asc. |
| tags | array(uuid) | Filter by an array of Tag UUID. |
| diseases | array(uuid) | Filter by an array of Diseases UUID |
| states | array(uuid) | Filter by an array of State UUID |
| case_type | string | Case Type can by New or Follow-up |
| is_sync | boolean | Searchs for Routine or Extended Care encounters. If missing, returns both. |
| is_live | boolean | Defines if the cases from live patients should be returned (defaults to true) |
| is_sandbox | boolean | Defines if the cases from sandbox patients should be returned (defaults to false) |
| is_additional_approval_needed | boolan | Search for cases that needs an additional approvel |

request.description:
Create a new case for a given patient. # Payload | field | type | description | | --- | --- | --- | | hold_status | boolean | Flag to hold the status to not enter into the MDI flow. It will be **false** by default. | | is_additional_approval_needed | boolean | It defines if the case needs an additional approve, | | reference_case_id | uuid | The referenced case UUID | | patient_id | uuid\* | Patient ID. Check **Partners > Cases > Patients** to create new patients | | metadata | string(255) | Field meant to store any information that helps identifying the case. (e.g., an ID that will be used to identify this case in another database) | | clinician_id | uuid | Clinician to assign the case to, in case there is a preference for a specific clinician in a specific case. If the field is not sent, a clinician will be assigned according to MDIntegrations algorithm. | | case_offerings | array(Offering) | List of offerings to be reviewed by a clinician | | diseases | array(Disease) | List of diseases to be attached to the case (ICD). | | case_files | array(uuid) | Files ID to be attached to the case. The files must be uploaded before creating a case. To upload files check **Partners > Files** | | case_questions | array(Question) | Questions and answers list | | is_chargeable | boolean | Whether or not the case should be charged | | tags | array(Tag) | List of Tags to be attached to the case. | ## Diseases Properties **Important**: Every disease object will add the disease with the ICD (International Classification of Disease) to the case. `[{disease_id}, {disease_id}]` **Note**: Use the "Get all Disease Codes" endpoint under the metadata folder to find a list of all availables UUID to be used. Once you know a disease id, you can also retrieve its information through the API using the Get Disease route. | field | type | description | | --- | --- | --- | | disease_id | UUID | Disease ID. It can be found through the diseases endpoints. | ## Tag Properties `[{tag_id}, {tag_id}]` **Note**: Use the "Get Tags" endpoint to find a list of all availables UUID to be used. Once you know a tag id, you can also retrieve its information through the API using the Get Tag route. | field | type | description | | --- | --- | --- | | tag_id | UUID | Tag UUID. It can be found through the tags endpoints. | ## Offering Properties **Note**: Please ask our medical team to add medications to your company's dashboard so it's easier to manage prescriptions details from there. | field | type | description | | --- | --- | --- | | offering_id | uuid | The offering ID. Check **Partners > Offerings** to get an `offering_id` for a given offering. **Required.** | | product | object | Specific offering fields to be overwritten when provided. Not required. Note each offering type has specific fields that can be overwritten with this payload. | | product.refills | integer (from 0 to 99) | Total of amount refills for the given offering | | product.quantity | string | Offering quantity string representing a float number (from 0 to 9999999999) | | product.days_supply | integer (from 0 to 365) | Number of days of supply of the offering provided - Allows `null` value | | product.directions | string(140) | Instructions about how the offering is administered | | product.pharmacy_notes | string(210) | Additional information about the offering | | product.no_substitutions | boolean | Whether or not an offering can be replaced to a similar formula/compound if needed. | | product.dispense_unit_id | integer | The dispense unit ID. For a list of all available dispense units check **Partners > Dispense Units**. \* | | product.pharmacy_id | integer | Pharmacy where the prescription must be sent to. Check **Partners > Pharmacies > Get pharmacies** to get a list of all pharmacies available.

**Important note**:
Dosespot works with **different IDs** for pharmacies according to the environment.
Example:
\- Curexa pharmacy ID in **staging** environment: 133012
\- Curexa pharmacy ID in **production** environment: 29992 | | product.effective_date | string | Date from which the offering will become fulfillable (time will always be 00:00:00). Format: `Y-m-d` | | title | string | Offering title | | product.name | string | Offering name | | order | integer | The offering ordering | ### Prescription Status Flow `pending`: When an offering is created on MDI side and not synced with Dosespot yet; `processing`: When MDI receives the Dosespot ID and send through the Dosespot the prescription to Pharmacy; `completed`: When Dosespot responds to the MDI that this prescription has been verified by the pharmacy; `cancelled`: When a doctor cancel the prescription (_when it was synced with Dosespot we try to cancel it there too_) # Question Properties For more information about `questions` and how they affect prescriptions, contact us via Slack or send an email to: [mserota@mdintegrations.com](https://mailto:mserota@mdintegrations.com) and [jeff@mdintegrations.com](https://mailto:jeff@mdintegrations.com) We are also available through Slack (partner's channel). | field | type | description | | --- | --- | --- | | question | string(255)\* | Question | | answer | text\* | Answer | | type | string\* | Question type. Available options: `boolean`, `number` or `string` | | important | boolean | Flag to set a question as `important`.

**Important Note**:
This flag is used to identify which questions could affect the final prescription. You could also implement custom logic to set this flag only when specific answers are selected. | | display_in_pdf | boolean | Flag to set if this question will be displayed in the case PDF file | | description | text | A description for your question | | label | string(50) | The question label | | metadata | text | A metadata field | | displayed_options | array | An array of options that were displayed to the patient | | is_critical | boolean | Mark question as critical |

item.description:
A **case** is as a set of medication prescriptions requests that the partner sends to MD-Integrations on behalf of a patient.

Once a case is created on MD Integrations side, it will be automatically assigned to a clinician who will be in charge of reviewing the case.

If everything is in place, the clinician will accept the case. By accepting the case, the requested prescription(s) will be sent to DoseSpot. On DoseSpot side the requested prescriptions will be created on behalf of the patient and sent to the given preferred pharmacy set.

## How To Create a Patient Case

#### 1\. Create a patient

Before creating a case, make sure the patient who the case will belongs to is registered on MD Integrations side. When a patient is created on MD Integrations an ID is returned. Make sure this ID is saved for later usage. For details about managing patients check **Partners > Patients**.

#### 2\. Search medications to be added to the case

There is no limit of medications to be added to a single case. To search for available medications, check **Partners > Medications > Search for medications**.

The medications search will only return the name and strength information when a medication is available. To get all the details for a medication, check  
**Partners > Medications > Get details for medications**.

#### 3\. Search compounds to be added to the case

There is no limit of compounds to be added to a single case. To search for available compounds, check **Partners > Compounds > Search for compounds**.

#### 4\. Search pharmacies to add as preferred for a given medication

Each medication added can be associated with a preferred pharmacy. When the medication is prescribed to the patient on MD Integrations, the prescription will be sent to the chosen pharmacy. To search for available pharmacies, check **Partners > Pharmacies**.

#### 5\. Attach files to the case

You can associate files to cases. First, you'll need to upload the files before creating the case. Check **Partners > Files > Create file** for more details.

#### 6\. Create case

Now it's time to submit the patient case using all the information gathered from the previous steps. To create a case check **Partners > Cases** for details about the request to be sent.

## Case Status

![](https://i.imgur.com/syqWaVo.png)

A case can have one of the following status:

*   **created**: a case is created using the created status;
*   **assigned**: indicates a case is ready to be worked by the assigned clinician;
*   **support**: the MD clinician transferred the case to support;
*   **waiting**: case is still assigned and waiting for a response from the patient (no staff or clinician action required).
*   **processing**: indicates that a case has been submitted to Dosespot and is waiting for confirmation
*   **completed**: a case is completed when all the case prescriptions are created on DoseSpot side and are automatically sent to the pharmacies; or moved manually without prescriptions.
*   **cancelled**: a case goes to cancelled when manually set by the clinician or support;;
    

## Webhooks

The Webhooks API allows you to subscribe to events happening on patient's cases for a specific MD Integrations partner.

After you've subscribed to a webhook, you can let your app execute code immediately after some specific events. The following events are triggered by MD Integrations:

*   Patient case has been assigned to a clinician;
*   Patient case has been processing;
*   Patient case has been completed;
*   Patient case has been transferred to support;
*   Patient case has a new message from the clinician.
*   Patient case has been cancelled;
*   Patient case has been waiting;
*   Patient has been modified;
*   Patient case prescriptions have been submitted;
    

#### Webhooks Setup

Using the Webhooks API requires the following:

*   Enter MD Integrations Admin Panel and go to **Settings**. There you will be able to specify a URL to send notifications;
*   The specified URL must be a publicly available, secure (HTTPS) endpoint.
    

#### Webhooks Payloads

The endpoint at the URL you specify in webhooks settings will receive `POST` requests with content type `application/json`.

Depending on the event type triggered, a different payload will be returned. The available events and each corresponding payload are the following:

**Patient case has been assigned to a clinician**

Once a patient case is created on MD Integrations, this case will be automatically assigned to a clinician. Once a case is assigned to a clinician, the case enters the clinician's queue to be reviewed.

```
{
  "event_type": "case_assigned_to_clinician",
  "case_id": "08e58832-4ae4-47e4-b9be-43dba87959bc",
  "metadata": "this is an example."
}

```

**Patient case has been completed**

A case is completed when all the case prescriptions are created on DoseSpot side and are automatically sent to the pharmacies; or moved manually without prescriptions.

```
{
  "event_type": "case_completed",
  "case_id": "08e58832-4ae4-47e4-b9be-43dba87959bc",
  "metadata": "this is an example."
}

```

**Patient case has been transferred to support**

In case the clinician has identified any issues with the patient's case, the clinician can transfer the case to the partner's support. The case then is flagged as sent to support on MD Integrations side and an event is emitted to the partner notifying that a case needs some support assistance.

```
{
  "event_type": "case_transferred_to_support",
  "case_id": "08e58832-4ae4-47e4-b9be-43dba87959bc",
  "metadata": "this is an example."
}

```

**Patient case has a new message from the clinician**

Patients and clinicians can exchange messages while the case is opened. When a clinician sends a message to the patient, this event is emitted.

```
{
  "event_type": "new_case_message",
  "case_id": "08e58832-4ae4-47e4-b9be-43dba87959bc",
  "case_message_id": "7d6bc4aa-73a1-42bf-bff8-1792d876566d",
  "metadata": "this is an example."
}

```

**Patient case has been processing**

Indicates that a case has been submitted to Dosespot and is waiting for confirmation.

```
{
  "event_type": "case_processing",
  "case_id": "08e58832-4ae4-47e4-b9be-43dba87959bc",
  "metadata": "this is an example."
}

```

**Patient case has been cancelled**

A case goes to cancelled when manually set by the clinician or support; A cancelled case can't be modified at all.

```
{
  "event_type": "case_cancelled",
  "case_id": "08e58832-4ae4-47e4-b9be-43dba87959bc",
  "metadata": "this is an example."
}

```

**Patient case has been waiting**

Waiting cases indicate that the clinician is waiting for a patient action (reply to a message, attach a blood exam, etc)

```
{
  "event_type": "case_waiting",
  "case_id": "08e58832-4ae4-47e4-b9be-43dba87959bc",
  "metadata": "this is an example."
}

```

**Patient has been modified**

Waiting cases indicate that the clinician is waiting for a patient action (reply to a message, attach a blood exam, etc)

```
{
  "event_type": "patient_modified",
  "patient_id": "08e58832-4ae4-47e4-b9be-43dba87959bc",
  "metadata": "this is an example."
}

```

**Patient case prescriptions have been submitted**

When a case is completed shows information about the prescriptions received by dosespot

```
{
  event_type: 'prescription_submitted',
  case_id: 'be2fc48f-df75-4cd7-a78a-89d51cc46e17',
  metadata: 'asdadssa',
  prescriptions: [
    {
      dosespot_prescription_id: null,
      refills: 2,
      quantity: 99,
      days_supply: 30,
      directions: 'Take one at the morning and another before bed...',
      dosespot_prescription_sync_status: 'pending',
      dosespot_sent_pharmacy_sync_status: 'pending',
      no_substitutions: true,
      pharmacy_notes: 'This medication might not be suitable for people with...',
      dosespot_confirmation_status: 'pending',
      dosespot_confirmation_status_details: null,
      thank_you_note: 'lorem ipsum',
      clinical_note: 'lorem ipsum',
      dispense_unit_id: 15,
      pharmacy_id: 13012,
      medication: [Object],
      partner_compound: null
    }
  ]
}

```

I’ve reviewed your health information and I think you likely have ED, which can benefit from the following:   Sildenafil 20mg tablets.  Start with 1-2 tablets and do not exceed a maximum of 100 mg of sildenafil (5 tablets) per day based on how you respond to treatment.\r\n\r\nThe treatment plan including other important information is below  Please make sure you review it, as it will help you decide if the benefits of the plan outweigh the risks.\r\n\r\nDon’t forget that there is always a package insert that comes with your prescription which you should review.\r\n\r\nPlease feel free to contact me here directly if you have any medical questions.\r\n\r\nSincerely,\r\nDr. Thomas Bottoni MD \r\nBoard-certified physician licensed in Florida \r\n\r\nHow to use the medication: Start with two tablets of the medication by mouth for your first time one hour before sex as needed.  You may then go up by 1 tablet each future sexual encounter if you feel you need to.  DO NOT TAKE MORE THAN FIVE TABLETS AT A TIME OR WITHIN A 24 HOUR PERIOD.\r\n\r\nDo not use this in combination with any illegal drugs. Taking with alcohol may make the medication less effective and increase the chances of side effects such as headache or flushing. Never take erectile dysfunction medication with nitrates for chest pain.\r\n\r\nSIDE EFFECTS: Here's your reference chart - don't panic if you experience these. They're ultra-normal. Most side effects only last as long as the medication is still in the system. That means it's a shorter duration for Viagra, Sildenafil, and Levitra...but longer for Cialis.\r\n \r\nSymptom\t\t\t\tFrequency\r\n* Headache                        16-28% vs 7% for placebo                \r\n* Upset stomach               10-29% vs 2% for placebo\r\n* Flushing                           10-29% vs 2% for placebo\r\n* Stuffy nose                       4% vs 2% for placebo\r\n* Dizziness.                         3-4% vs 2% for placebo\r\n* Muscle aches                   3-4% vs 2% for placebo\r\n* Dyspepsia (‘reflux’)         3-17% vs 2% for placebo\r\n\r\nLess common side effects include:\r\n* Rash\r\n* Abnormal vision (blue tint, blurry vision light sensitivity)\r\n\r\nSevere Side Effects (very uncommon)\r\n* Erections that last several hours. Tell your doctor right away if you have an erection that lasts more than 4 hours.\r\n* Decreased eyesight or loss of sight in one or both eyes.\r\n* Sudden decrease or loss of hearing.\r\n* Heart attack, stroke, irregular heartbeats, and death. Most of these happened in people who already had heart problems.\r\n* Seizures\r\n* Low blood pressure causing dizziness\r\n* Allergic reaction\r\n* Stevens-Johnson syndrome\r\n* Anemia\r\nIf any of these SEVERE side effects occur, seek medical care immediately.\r\n\r\nWe require that you continue to follow up regularly (at least once a year) with your primary care doctor for in person check ups. It is important that you inform them of your ED symptoms and treatment so that they can investigate any additional contributors and optimize treatment for any underlying conditions/triggers which may include: heart disease, atherosclerosis, high cholesterol, high blood pressure, diabetes, obesity, metabolic syndrome, Parkinson’s disease, multiple sclerosis, sleep apnea, prostate disease, other GU diseases, low testosterone, psychological conditions, certain medications, smoking, drug and alcohol use.  Please remember to let us know of any changes in your health. If you have any anatomic problems or urology surgeries that could impact erectile dysfunction or if there is any change to your cardiovascular health please always let myself and your primary doctor know as this may impact the treatment recommendations for your ED.\r\n\r\nFOR OTHER HELPFUL INFORMATION INCLUDING ALTERNATIVES TO THERAPY, please read the following: https://support.scoreblue.com/hc/en-us  \r\n\r\nIf you do not have a PCP, please click on the following link: https://www.google.com/search?q=primary+care+doctor+near+me",

| files | array |
| sender_type | string (patient/partner/system) |

# Payload (files)

| **field** | **type** |
| --- | --- |
| id\* | uuid |

Holcomb, M.D. is the author of more than 60 papers on clinical interests that include psoriasis, acne, melanoma, tropical dermatology, and photosensitive skin disorders. Her work has been published in the International Journal of Dermatology, Dermatology Online Journal, Journal of Cutaneous Pathology, and other dermatological publications, including numerous Continuing Medical Education articles for ClinicalAdvisor.com’s Dermatology Clinic. Dr. Holcomb has also collaborated on several presentations of dermatological research at conferences of the Texas Dermatological Society, American Society of \r\n dermatopathology, and American Academy of Dermatology.\r\n\r\nA native Texan, Dr. Holcomb graduated at the top of her high school class in East Texas before attending Texas A&M University, where she graduated magna cum laude, obtaining degrees in\r\nboth biology and psychology. \r\n\r\nDr. Holcomb received her M.D. from Baylor College of Medicine on a full scholarship. She completed her medical internship at The Methodist Hospital in Houston and her dermatology residency at Baylor. She conducted clinical research at the Department of Dermatology at The University of Texas M.D. Anderson Cancer Center, Houston, and received the Debra Chester Kalter Award for Outstanding Student Research in Dermatology. Graduating in the top 10% of her class, she was elected into the Alpha Omega Alpha Medical Honor Society.\r\n\r\nDuring medical school, Dr. Holcomb spent two months volunteering at a leprosy hospital in Nepal. In her spare time, she enjoys hiking, running, camping, travelling off the beaten path, and scuba diving.",

item.description:
# Messages Source

A message can be sent from the following sources and you can find who sent the message through the `user_type` parameter.

- **support**: message sent by support;  
    The `user_type` will be `App\Models\SupportStaff`
- **patient**: message sent by the patient;  
    The `user_type` will be `App\Models\Patient`
- **clinician**: message sent by the clinician.  
    The `user_type` will be `App\Models\Clinician`
    

You can check the field `user_type` from the payload response to get who sent the message.

### User object

The `user` object is related to the user that sent the message (_user from message_).

This object is mutable depending on the `user_type` However, there are some parameters in common, for example:

- `user.id`
- `users.first_name`
- `user.last_name`
- `user.email`
    

Note that many other parameters will change considerably since they are totally different user types.

For example, a `Patient` could have a `driver_license_id` whereas a `Clinician` or a `Support` does not.

### Dismissed by object

It works exactly the same way for the `dismissed_by_type` `dismissed_by_id` `dismissed_by` parameter that is related to the user that dismissed the message.

# Messages Channel

A message can be sent to the following channels:

- **patient**: channel for exchanging messages between patient and clinicians;
- **support**: channel for exchanging messages between support and clinicians.
    

You can check the field `channel` from the payload response to get the corresponding message channel.

Messages originated from patients are automatically sent to **patient** channel while messages from support are automatically sent to **support** channel.

request.description:
Adds a pharmacy to a patient’s preferred pharmacies list.

To check a list of available pharmacies to be added to the patient, check **Partners > Pharmacies**.

query.description: You can choose the sorting between store_name or updated_at. 'store_name' is alphabetical and 'updated_at' is based on the date and time of linking to the patient.
You can choose the ordering between asc or desc.

request.description: Retrieves a detailed list of preferred pharmacies in the patient’s record. This API endpoint forwards the request to DoseSpot API in order to retrieve this information.

request.description:
Removes a pharmacy from a patient’s preferred pharmacies list. This API endpoint forwards the request to DoseSpot API in order to remove patient's pharmacies.

This does not change any existing medications in that patient’s record even if they are queued up or already sent to that pharmacy.

If patient exists with the email informed then we send a verification code to the patient email.

| **param** | **type** | **description** |
| --- | --- | --- |
| email | string\* | Patient's email |

This endpoint validates the verification code sent to the patient's email.

If the code is validated then we return the patient object.

request.description:
Creates a new patient record both on MD Integrations and DoseSpot. # Payload | field | type | description | | --- | --- | --- | | prefix | string(10) | Prefix | | first_name | string(35)\* | First name | | last_name | string(35)\* | Last name | | ssn | string(11) | Security Social Number.
**Format**: `AAAGGSSSS` | | email | string(80)\* | E-mail | | date_of_birth | date\* | Date of birth, as YYYY-MM-DD | | driver_license_id | uuid | Patient's Driver License, needs to reference an existing **File**. Created through **Files > Create file**. | | gender | integer\* | Gender (ISO 5218 format) | | metadata | string(255) | Field meant to store any information that helps identifying the patient. (e.g., an ID that will be used to identify this patient in another database) | | phone_number | string(25)\* | Phone number - _US format_ | | phone_type | integer\* | Phone type. Available options: 2 (cell) or 4 (home) | | address.address | string(35)\* | Address | | address.address2 | string(35) | Additional address - Allows `null` value | | address.zip_code | string(9)\* | US zip code - _5 or 9 digit format
OR
_CA zip code - 6 alphanumeric characters | | address.city_name | string(35)\* | City name. | | address.state_name | string\* | State name or State USPS abbreviation. | | weight | float (from 0 to 999) | Weight in KG \* _required if patient is underage_ | | height | integer (from 0 to 300) | Height in CM \* _required if patient is underage_ | | allergies | text | Known patient's allergies | | pregnancy | boolean | Define the patient with a pregnancy | | current_medications | text | Medications that the patient is currently taking | | medical_conditions | text | Medical conditions related to the patient | | special_necessities | text | Special necessities related to the patient | | intro_video_id | uuid | Patient's Intro Video, needs to reference an existing **File**. Created through **Files > Create file**. | | metafields | array | Patient custom properties | | metafields.\*.key | string\* | Field key | | metafields.\*.title | string | Field title (label) | | metafields.\*.value | string\* | Field value | | metafields.\*.type | string\* | Field type |

request.description:
Update a patient record both on MD Integrations and DoseSpot. # Payload | field | type | description | | --- | --- | --- | | prefix | string(10) | Prefix | | first_name | string(35) | First name | | last_name | string(35) | Last name | | ssn | string(11) | Security Social Number.
**Format**: `AAAGGSSSS` | | email | string(80) | E-mail | | date_of_birth | date | Date of birth, as YYYY-MM-DD | | driver_license_id | uuid | Patient's Driver License, needs to reference an existing **File**. Created through **Files > Create file**. | | metadata | string(255) | Field meant to store any information that helps identifying the patient. (e.g., an ID that will be used to identify this patient in another database) | | gender | integer | Gender (ISO 5218 format) | | phone_number | string(25) | Phone number - _US format_ | | phone_type | integer | Phone type. Available options: 2 (cell) or 4 (home) | | address.address | string(35) | Address | | address.address2 | string(35) | Additional address - Allows `null` value | | address.zip_code | string(9) | US zip code - _5 or 9 digit format
OR
_CA zip code - 6 alphanumeric characters | | address.city_id | uuid | City ID. Check **Metadata > States > Cities> Get cities from a given state** to get city ID. If you use this parameter you don't need to use **city_name** parameter. | | address.city_name | string(35) | City name. If you use this parameter you don't need use **city_id** parameter. | | address.state_name | string | State name or State USPS abbreviation. If you use this parameter you don't need to use **city_id** parameter. | | weight | float (from 0 to 999) | Weight in KG \* _required if patient is underage_ | | height | integer (from 0 to 300) | Height in CM \* _required if patient is underage_ | | allergies | text | Known patient's allergies | | pregnancy | boolean | Define the patient with a pregnancy | | current_medications | text | Medications that the patient is currently taking | | medical_conditions | text | A medical condition information. | | special_necessities | text | A special necessities related to patient | | intro_video_id | uuid | Patient's Intro Video, needs to reference an existing **File**. Created through **Files > Create file**. |

item.description:
The files endpoints from MD Integrations allows you to easily upload, get and delete image files.

If you want to **attach files to cases** or **attach files to messages**, first you will need to upload the file using `POST /partners/files` described in this section. After uploading a file, you will obtain the `file_id` information, which is the required information in order to attach files to cases or messages.

## Attach Files To Cases

Files can be attached to cases through **Partners > Cases > Files**.

## Attach Files To Messages

Files can be attached to messages through **Partners > Cases > Messages**.

## Response

The request response to upload a file contains the following information:

- `file_id`: corresponding created file ID. This information is used to attach files to cases or messages;
- `name`: file name;
- `url`: URL from original file;
- `url_thumbnail`: URL from thumbnail image with **180x110px**.

request.description:
Return voucher data for a voucher ID.

You may find additional details on embedding at [this](https://galactic-shadow-134413.postman.co/workspace/MD-Integrations~0bf566d7-bf01-4290-ab8e-1f3cf71a8da6/request/14212272-98004812-41bd-4771-915e-180ec888d420?action=share&source=copy-link&creator=14212272&ctx=documentation) documentation.

response.name: Success

request.description:
Creates a new voucher. ## Payload **Important**: when creating a voucher you can specify the medications, compounds and services to be used. | field | type | description | | --- | --- | --- | | **questionnaire_id** | **uuid**\* | Partner Questionnaire ID. It can be found on your company's dashboard or using **Patient App > Questionnaires > List questionnaires** | | patient_id | uuid | Patient ID. | | hold_status | boolean | Flag to hold the status to not enter into the MDI flow. It will be **false** by default. | | demo | boolean | Demo vouchers will not create any patient or cases and will not expire. Default `false` | | expires_at | datetime | If not passed then the default will be a month ahead. | | offerings | array | It can be empty | | offerings**.\*.**id | uuid\* | The offering ID. Check **Partners > Offerings** to get an `offering_id` for a given offering. Required if is present. | | offerings.\*.product | object | Contains fields that are specific to each offering type, such as pharmacy_id or force_pharmacy. | | offerings.\*.product.pharmacy_id | integer\* | The pharmacy id you would like to use for the specific offering. Only applies to Dosespot offerings. | | offerings.\*.product.force_pharmacy | boolean\* | Determines whether we should apply the selected pharmacy_id (from payload or offering) regardless if user selects a different pharmacy on the intake form. Only applies to Dosespot offerings. | | diseases | array | it can be empty or null. | | diseases.\*.disease_id | uuid | Disease ID. It can be found on your company's dashboard or through the diseases endpoint. Nullable. | | metadata | string (255) | Metadata value. It can be empty or null. | | pharmacy_id | integer | he Dosespot Pharmacy ID.

Check **Partners** > **Pharmacies** > **Get pharmacies** to get a list of all pharmacies available. | You may find additional details on embedding [here](https://documenter.getpostman.com/view/14212272/2s8Yt1r9B8#9170cf8f-851d-466d-9202-0139a532c18f).

response.name: Success

request.description:
Create a new encounter for a given patient. # Payload | field | type | description | | --- | --- | --- | | patient_id | uuid\* | Patient ID. Check **Partners > Cases > Patients** to create new patients | | reference_case_id | uuid | The referenced case UUID | | metadata | string(255) | Field meant to store any information that helps identifying the case. (e.g., an ID that will be used to identify this case in another database) | | clinician_id | uuid\* | Clinician to assign the case to, in case there is a preference for a specific clinician in a specific case. If the field is not sent, a clinician will be assigned according to MDIntegrations algorithm. | | case_prescriptions | array(Prescription) | List of prescriptions to be reviewed by a clinician | | case_services | array(Service) | List of services to be reviewed by a clinician | | case_files | array(uuid) | Files ID to be attached to the case. The files must be uploaded before creating a case. To upload files check **Partners > Files** | | case_questions | array(Question) | Questions and answers list | | is_chargeable | boolean | Whether or not the case should be charged | | tags | array(object(uuid)) | Tags ID to be attached to the case. | ## Prescription Properties **Important**: a prescription object must always contain **exactly one** drug type ID from the following: \[`partner_medication_id`, `partner_compound_id`, `partner_supply_id`, `dosespot_supply_ndc`\] **Note**: We strongly advise asking our medical team to add medications to your company's dashboard so it's easier to manage prescriptions details from there. Should you need to add a one-time-only medication, you can use the dosespot_medication_id to reference a Dosespot medication directly. | field | type | description | | --- | --- | --- | | partner_compound_id | uuid | Partner compound ID. Check **Partners > Compounds** to get a `partner_compound_id` for a given compound. **Required if** `dosespot_medication_id` and `partner_supply_id` and `dosespot_supply_ndc` are `null` | | partner_medication_id | uuid | Partner medication ID. It can be found on your company's dashboard. **Required if** `dosespot_medication_id` and `partner_medication_id` and `partner_supply_id` and `dosespot_supply_ndc` are `null` | | partner_supply_id | uuid | Partner supply ID. It can be found on your company's dashboard. **Required if** `dosespot_medication_id` and `partner_compound_id` and `partner_medication_id` and `dosespot_supply_ndc` are `null` | | dosespot_supply_ndc | string | Dosespot Supply NDC code. **Required if** `dosespot_medication_id` and `partner_compound_id` and `partner_medication_id` and `partner_supply_id` are `null` | | refills | integer (from 0 to 99) | Total of refills for the given medication \* **Required if** `dosespot_medication_id` or `dosespot_supply_ndc` is present | | quantity | string | Medication quantity string representing a float number (from 0 to 9999999999) **Required if** `dosespot_medication_id` or `dosespot_supply_ndc` is present | | days_supply | integer (from 0 to 365) | Number of days of supply of the medication provided - Allows `null` value | | directions | string(140) | Instructions about how the medication is administered **Required if** `dosespot_medication_id` or `dosespot_supply_ndc` is present | | pharmacy_notes | string(210) | Additional information about the prescription | | no_substitutions | boolean | Whether or not a medication or compound can be changed | | dispense_unit_id | integer | \- **Medication** (prescription with a `dosespot_medication_id` included): each medication has one associated dispense unit ID. To get the dispense unit ID associated with a given medication check **Partners > Medications > Get details for a specific medication**;

\- **Compound** (prescription with `partner_compound_id` included): compounds can be associated with any available dispense unit ID. For a list of all available dispense units check **Partners > Dispense Units**. \* **Required if** `dosespot_medication_id` or `dosespot_supply_ndc` is present | | pharmacy_id | integer | Pharmacy where the prescription must be sent to. Check **Partners > Pharmacies > Get pharmacies** to get a list of all pharmacies available.

**Important note**:

Dosespot works with **different IDs** for pharmacies according to the environment.
Example:
\- Curexa pharmacy ID in **staging** environment: 133012
\- Curexa pharmacy ID in **production** environment: 29992 | | effective_date | string | Date from which prescription is effective (time will always be 00:00:00). Format: `Y-m-d` | ### Prescription Status Flow `pending`: When prescription is created on MDI side and not synced with Dosespot yet; `processing`: When MDI receives the Dosespot ID and send through the Dosespot the prescription to Pharmacy; `completed`: When Dosespot responds to the MDI that this prescription has been verified by the pharmacy; `cancelled`: When a doctor cancel the prescription (_when it was synced with Dosespot we try to cancel it there too_) # Question Properties For more information about `questions` and how they affect prescriptions, contact us via Slack or send an email to: [mserota@mdintegrations.com](https://mailto:mserota@mdintegrations.com) and [jeff@mdintegrations.com](https://mailto:jeff@mdintegrations.com) We are also available through Slack (partner's channel). | field | type | description | | --- | --- | --- | | question | string(255)\* | Question | | answer | text\* | Answer | | type | string\* | Question type. Available options: `boolean`, `number` or `string` | | important | boolean | Flag to set a question as `important`.

**Important Note**:
This flag is used to identify which questions could affect the final prescription. You could also implement custom logic to set this flag only when specific answers are selected. | | display_in_pdf | boolean | Flag to set if this question will be displayed in the case PDF file | | description | text | A description for your question | | label | string(50) | The question label | | metadata | text | A metadata field | | displayed_options | array | An array of options that were displayed to the patient | ## Service Properties **Important**: a service object can include a **partner_service_id** in order to use predefined information from a partner service. **Note**: Partner services can be found under your company's offerings from the Partners Website. Once you know a partner's service id, you can also retrieve its information through the api using the **Get Service** route. | **field** | **type** | | --- | --- | | title | string (**required if partner_service_id is NOT included)** | | description | string (**required if partner_service_id is NOT included)** | | service_type | string (**required if partner_service_id is NOT included)** | | additional_note | string | | partner_service_id | uuid | | clinical_notes | string | | thank_you_note | string | | pharmacy_fax_number | string | | include_drivers_license | boolean | | send_pdf_file_automatically | boolean |

request.description:
Create a encounter prescription. # Payload Receives an object of **Prescription**. ## Prescription Properties **Important**: a prescription object must always contain **exactly one** drug type ID from the following: \[`partner_medication_id`, `partner_compound_id`, `partner_supply_id`, `dosespot_supply_ndc`\] **Note**: We strongly advise asking our medical team to add medications to your company's dashboard so it's easier to manage prescriptions details from there. Should you need to add a one-time-only medication, you can use the dosespot_medication_id to reference a Dosespot medication directly. | field | type | description | | --- | --- | --- | | clinician_id | uuid\* | Clinician to simulate the action | | partner_compound_id | uuid | Partner compound ID. Check **Partners > Compounds** to get a `partner_compound_id` for a given compound. **Required if** `dosespot_medication_id` and `partner_supply_id` and `dosespot_supply_ndc` are `null` | | partner_medication_id | uuid | Partner medication ID. It can be found under your company's offerings from the Partners Website. **Required if** `dosespot_medication_id` and `partner_medication_id` and `partner_supply_id` and `dosespot_supply_ndc` are `null` | | partner_supply_id | uuid | Partner supply ID. It can be found under your company's offerings from the Partners Website. **Required if** `dosespot_medication_id` and `partner_compound_id` and `partner_medication_id` and `dosespot_supply_ndc` are `null` | | dosespot_supply_ndc | string | Dosespot Supply NDC code. **Required if** `dosespot_medication_id` and `partner_compound_id` and `partner_medication_id` and `partner_supply_id` are `null` | | refills | integer (from 0 to 99) | Total of refills for the given medication \* Required if `dosespot_medication_id` is present | | quantity | string | Medication quantity string representing a float number (from 0 to 9999999999) **Required if** `dosespot_medication_id` is present | | days_supply | integer (from 0 to 365) | Number of days of supply of the medication provided - Allows `null` value | | directions | string(140) | Instructions about how the medication is administered **Required if** `dosespot_medication_id` is present | | pharmacy_notes | string(210) | Additional information about the prescription | | no_substitutions | boolean | Whether or not a medication or compound can be changed | | dispense_unit_id | integer | \- **Medication** (prescription with a `dosespot_medication_id` included): each medication has one associated dispense unit ID. To get the dispense unit ID associated with a given medication check **Partners > Medications > Get details for a specific medication**;

\- **Compound** (prescription with `partner_compound_id` included): compounds can be associated with any available dispense unit ID. For a list of all available dispense units check **Partners > Dispense Units**. \* Required if `dosespot_medication_id` is present | | pharmacy_id | integer\* | Pharmacy where the prescription must be sent to. Check **Partners > Pharmacies > Get pharmacies** to get a list of all pharmacies available | | effective_date | string | Date from which prescription is effective (time will always be 00:00:00). Format: `Y-m-d` | ### Prescription Status Flow `pending`: When prescription is created on MDI side and not synced with Dosespot yet; `processing`: When MDI receives the Dosespot ID and send through the Dosespot the prescription to Pharmacy; `completed`: When Dosespot responds to the MDI that this prescription has been verified by the pharmacy; `cancelled`: When a doctor cancel the prescription (_when it was synced with Dosespot we try to cancel it there too_)

request.description:
Create a new patient and encounter. # Case Payload | field | type | description | | --- | --- | --- | | preferred_pharmacy_id | integer | Dosespot Pharmacy ID | | questionnaire_id | uuid\* | Questionnaire ID | | completion_time | string\* | Time in the format "hh:mm:ss" | # Patient Payload | field | type | description | | --- | --- | --- | | prefix | string(10)\* | Prefix | | first_name | string(35)\* | First name | | last_name | string(35)\* | Last name | | ssn | string(11) | Security Social Number. **Format**: `AAAGGSSSS` | | email | string(80)\* | E-mail | | date_of_birth | date\* | Date of birth, as YYYY-MM-DD | | driver_license_id | uuid | Patient's Driver License, needs to reference an existing **File**. Created through **Files > Create file**. | | gender | integer\* | Gender (ISO 5218 format) | | metadata | string(255) | Field meant to store any information that helps identifying the patient. (e.g., an ID that will be used to identify this patient in another database) | | phone_number | string(25)\* | Phone number - _US format_ | | phone_type | integer\* | Phone type. Available options: 2 (cell) or 4 (home) | | address.address | string(35)\* | Address | | address.address2 | string(35) | Additional address - Allows `null` value | | address.zip_code | string(9)\* | US zip code - _5 or 9 digit format\*_OR\*CA zip code - 6 alphanumeric characters | | address.city_name | string(35)\* | City name. | | address.state_name | string\* | State name or State USPS abbreviation. | | weight | float (from 0 to 999) | Weight in KG \* _required if patient is underage_ | | height | integer (from 0 to 300) | Height in CM \* _required if patient is underage_ | | body_temperature | float (from 0.0 to 100.0) | Body temperature in Celsius | | oxygen_saturation | integer (from 0 to 100) | Oxygen Saturation in percentage | | special_necessities | text | Special necessities to be informed about in this case | | allergies | text | Known patient's allergies | | pregnancy | boolean | Define the patient with a pregnancy | | current_medications | text | Medications that the patient is currently taking | | intro_video_id | uuid | Patient's Intro Video, needs to reference an existing **File**. Created through **Files > Create file**. | # Case Payload | field | type | description | | --- | --- | --- | | metadata | string(255) | Field meant to store any information that helps identifying the case. (e.g., an ID that will be used to identify this case in another database) | | clinician_id | uuid | Clinician to assign the case to, in case there is a preference for a specific clinician in a specific case. If the field is not sent, a clinician will be assigned according to MDIntegrations algorithm. | | case_prescriptions | array(Prescription) | List of prescriptions to be reviewed by a clinician | | case_files | array(uuid) | Files ID to be attached to the case. The files must be uploaded before creating a case. To upload files check **Partners > Files** | | case_questions | array(Question) | Questions and answers list | | case_services | array(Services) | List of services to be reviewed by a clinician | | tags | array(uuid) | Tags ID to be attached to the case. | ## Prescription Properties **Important**: a prescription object must always contain **exactly one** drug type ID from the following: \[`partner_medication_id`, `partner_compound_id`, `partner_supply_id`\] **Note**: We strongly advise asking our medical team to add medications to your company's dashboard so it's easier to manage prescriptions details from there. Should you need to add a one-time-only medication, you can use the dosespot_medication_id to reference a Dosespot medication directly. | field | type | description | | --- | --- | --- | | partner_compound_id | uuid | Partner compound ID. Check **Partners > Compounds** to get a `partner_compound_id` for a given compound. **Required if** `dosespot_medication_id` and `partner_medication_id` are `null` | | partner_medication_id | uuid | Partner medication ID. It can be found under your company's offerings from the Partners Website. **Required if** `dosespot_medication_id` and `partner_compound_id` are `null` | | partner_supply_id | uuid | Partner supply ID. It can be found under your company's offerings from the Partners Website. **Required if** `dosespot_medication_id` and `partner_compound_id` and `partner_supply_id` are `null` | | refills | integer (from 0 to 99) | Total of refills for the given medication \* Required if `dosespot_medication_id` is present | | quantity | string | Medication quantity string representing a float number (from 0 to 9999999999) **Required if** `dosespot_medication_id` is present | | days_supply | integer (from 0 to 365) | Number of days of supply of the medication provided - Allows `null` value | | directions | string(140) | Instructions about how the medication is administered **Required if** `dosespot_medication_id` is present | | pharmacy_notes | string(210) | Additional information about the prescription | | no_substitutions | boolean | Whether or not a medication or compound can be changed | | dispense_unit_id | integer | \- **Medication** (prescription with a `dosespot_medication_id` included): each medication has one associated dispense unit ID. To get the dispense unit ID associated with a given medication check **Partners > Medications > Get details for a specific medication**;

\- **Compound** (prescription with `partner_compound_id` included): compounds can be associated with any available dispense unit ID. For a list of all available dispense units check **Partners > Dispense Units**. \* Required if `dosespot_medication_id` is present | | pharmacy_id | integer\* | Pharmacy where the prescription must be sent to. Check **Partners > Pharmacies > Get pharmacies** to get a list of all pharmacies available.

**Required if**: `dosespot_medication_id` is present.
**Required if**: `partner_medication_id` or `partner_medication_id` does not have a registered default pharmacy.

**Important note**:

Dosespot works with **different IDs** for pharmacies according to the environment.
Example:
\- Curexa pharmacy ID in **staging** environment: 133012
\- Curexa pharmacy ID in **production** environment: 29992 | | effective_date | string | Date from which prescription is effective (time will always be 00:00:00). Format: `Y-m-d` | # Question Properties For more information about `questions` and how they affect prescriptions, contact us via Slack or send an email to: [mserota@mdintegrations.com](https://mailto:mserota@mdintegrations.com) and [jeff@mdintegrations.com](https://mailto:jeff@mdintegrations.com) We are also available through Slack (partner's channel). | field | type | description | | --- | --- | --- | | question | string(255)\* | Question | | answer | text\* | Answer | | type | string\* | Question type. Available options: `boolean`, `number` or `string` | | important | boolean | Flag to set a question as `important`.

**Important Note**:
This flag is used to identify which questions could affect the final prescription. You could also implement custom logic to set this flag only when specific answers are selected. | | display_in_pdf | boolean | Flag to set if this question will be displayed in the case PDF file | # Diseases **Important**: Every disease object will add the disease with the ICD (International Classification of Disease) to the case. **Note**: Diseses can be found on the diseases page on the Admin panel. Once you know a disease id, you can also retrieve its information through the API using the Get Disease route. | field | type | description | | --- | --- | --- | | disease_id | UUID | Disease ID. It can be found through the diseases endpoints. | ## Service Properties **Important**: a service object can include a **partner_service_id** in order to use predefined information from a partner service. **Note**: Partner services can be found under your company's offerings from the Partners Website. Once you know a partner's service id, you can also retrieve its information through the api using the **Get Service** route. | **field** | **type** | | --- | --- | | title | string (**required if partner_service_id is NOT included)** | | description | string (**required if partner_service_id is NOT included)** | | service_type | string (**required if partner_service_id is NOT included)** | | additional_note | string | | partner_service_id | uuid | | clinical_notes | string | | thank_you_note | string | | pharmacy_fax_number | string | | include_drivers_license | boolean | | send_pdf_file_automatically | boolean |

request.description:
The following documentation applies to all white label experiences. Some of this functionality might be limited to certain events.

``` html
<iframe src="{{ mdi_url }}" allow="camera; microphone"></iframe>

 ```

## Embedded Form

If you plan on embedding the intake form on your own page, you may append the following query parameter at the end of the URL provided on workflow request responses to remove borders, branding and other visual elements for a seamless experience:

| param | type | description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **fullscreen** | boolean\* | If set to TRUE will remove borders, logos and other visual elements from around the intake form window to make it seamless when embedding it. |

You may also subscribe to [window postMessage events](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/Window/postMessage) to keep track of important events and user progress. Different workflows may trigger different events.

| event | data | description | **scope** |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| load | \- | White Label App loaded | all |
| unload | \- | User is attempting to close the screen | all |
| start | \- | User has started filling out the questionnaire | intake form |
| finish | \- | User has finished the intake form | intake form |
| step | step, index, estimated, total, question_id\* | User has progressed through a step. It can either be a demographic step or customized question | intake form |
| logged_in | patient_id | User has logged in | all |
| encounter_created | encounter_id, patient_id | Encounter created | intake form |
| patient_created | patient_id | Patient created | intake form |
| skipped_question | \- | User has skipped a question and progressed through a step. | intake form |
| dead_end_question | \- | User has reached a question that blocks her from progressing on the intake. | intake form |
| otp_ready¹ | \- | User has reached the OTP screen | all |
| otp_failed¹ | \- | Provided OTP is invalid | all |

An embedded white label app will also support certain events and messages:

| **message** | **data** | **description** | **scope** |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| otp¹ | _string_ | Sends in an OTP to be inputted and auto submits the form. Use this message to create a seamless login experience on workflows that require additional authentication | OTP pages |

The following sample showcases how one of the events above would be sent client-side to an iframe with one of our white label experiences embedded:

``` typescript
const payload = {
  message: 'otp',
  data: 'ABCDEF',
};
iFrame?.contentWindow?.postMessage(payload, '*');

 ```

Note by Nov. 12th we will no longer return pre-authenticated URLs for the white label workflows², effectively requiring an OTP code to be inserted manually by the user. If you are embedding them on your website and want to keep a seamless login experience, you should listen for a `otp_ready` event from the iframe and send an `otp` message with the verification_code returned by the workflow endpoint like so:

``` typescript
window.addEventListener('message', (e) => {
  const event = e.data.event;
  if (event === 'otp_ready') {
    iframe.contentWindow.postMessage({ message: 'otp', data: verification_code }, '*');
  }
});

 ```

¹: Feature will only be available after Nov 12th, 2024.  
²: Intake Forms are excluded from this change.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: bearer

request.description:
With this endpoint, you can exchange your **Client ID** and **Client Secret** for an access token with _limited lifetime_.

The access token allows your application to make authenticated requests to the MD Integrations API.

For more details on the expected flow, you can checkout the **Authentication** section in the **Introduction**.

## Payload

For this request, all fields in the payload are **required**.

| field | type | description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| grant_type | string\* | Should always be `client_credentials` |
| client_id | string\* | Client ID credential |
| client_secret | string\* | Client Secret credential |
| scope | string\* | Should always be `\*` |

## Expired tokens

Whenever an access token expires, you should request a brand new one for making new requests.

## Client Credentials

Your credentials will be provided to you by the MD Integrations team.

Keep in mind the importance of **protecting your credentials** to avoid unauthorized access.

response.name: Success

item.description:
# MD Integrations API for Partners

## Intro

The MD Integrations API for partners is a set of HTTP endpoints that allows partners to perform all the required actions in order to create cases for patients on MD Integrations.

item.description:
## Webhook Events

Our API will send information to your application about events that occur on the platform.

#### **Retry**

If your endpoint does not return an **HTTP 200** response within **15 seconds**, the delivery attempt will be considered failed and will automatically **trigger** the retry flow.

#### Timeout

- **Webhook Timeout:** 15 seconds.
    

#### Retry Strategy

When a webhook delivery fails, we will attempt to resend it according to the schedule below:

| **Attempt** | **Delay after failure** |
| --- | --- |
| **1st retry** | 15 minutes |
| **2nd retry** | 30 minutes |
| **3rd retry** | 1 hour |
| **4th retry** | 4 hours |
| **5th retry** | 8 hours |
| **6th retry** | 11 hours |

**Important note**: Webhook delivery attempts will occur over a **24-hour period**. If all 6 retry attempts fail, the webhook will be marked as permanently failed.

request.description:
Create a new case. # Case Payload | field | type | description | | --- | --- | --- | | metadata | string(255) | Field meant to store any information that helps identifying the case. (e.g., an ID that will be used to identify this case in another database) | | clinician_id | uuid | Clinician to assign the case to, in case there is a preference for a specific clinician in a specific case. If the field is not sent, a clinician will be assigned according to MD Integrations algorithm. | | case_prescriptions | array(Prescription) | List of prescriptions to be reviewed by a clinician | | case_files | array(uuid) | Files ID to be attached to the case. The files must be uploaded before creating a case. To upload files check **Partners > Files** | | case_questions | array(Question) | Questions and answers list | | case_services | array(Services) | List of services to be reviewed by a clinician | | tags | array(uuid) | Tags ID to be attached to the case. | | is_additional_approval_needed | boolean | If the case needs an additional approval | ## Prescription Properties **Important**: a prescription object must always contain **exactly one** drug type ID from the following: \[`partner_medication_id`, `partner_compound_id`, `partner_supply_id`\] **Note**: We strongly advise to add a medication to the Admin Panel so it's easier to manage a prescription's details from there. Should you need to add a one-time-only medication, you can use the dosespot_medication_id to reference a Dosespot medication directly. | field | type | description | | --- | --- | --- | | partner_compound_id | uuid | Partner compound ID. Check **Partners > Compounds** to get a `partner_compound_id` for a given compound. **Required if** `dosespot_medication_id` and `partner_medication_id` are `null` | | partner_medication_id | uuid | Partner medication ID. It can be found at the Admin Panel. **Required if** `dosespot_medication_id` and `partner_compound_id` are `null` | | partner_supply_id | uuid | Partner supply ID. It can be found at the Admin Panel. **Required if** `dosespot_medication_id` and `partner_compound_id` and `partner_supply_id` are `null` | | refills | integer (from 0 to 99) | Total of refills for the given medication \* Required if `dosespot_medication_id` is present | | quantity | string | Medication quantity string representing a float number (from 0 to 9999999999) **Required if** `dosespot_medication_id` is present | | days_supply | integer (from 0 to 365) | Number of days of supply of the medication provided - Allows `null` value | | directions | string(140) | Instructions about how the medication is administered **Required if** `dosespot_medication_id` is present | | pharmacy_notes | string(210) | Additional information about the prescription | | no_substitutions | boolean | Whether or not a medication or compound can be changed | | dispense_unit_id | integer | \- **Medication** (prescription with a `dosespot_medication_id` included): each medication has one associated dispense unit ID. To get the dispense unit ID associated with a given medication check **Partners > Medications > Get details for a specific medication**;

\- **Compound** (prescription with `partner_compound_id` included): compounds can be associated with any available dispense unit ID. For a list of all available dispense units check **Partners > Dispense Units**. \* Required if `dosespot_medication_id` is present | | pharmacy_id | integer\* | Pharmacy where the prescription must be sent to. Check **Partners > Pharmacies > Get pharmacies** to get a list of all pharmacies available.

**Required if**: `dosespot_medication_id` is present.
**Required if**: `partner_medication_id` or `partner_medication_id` does not have a registered default pharmacy.

**Important note**:

Dosespot works with **different IDs** for pharmacies according to the environment.
Example:
\- Curexa pharmacy ID in **staging** environment: 133012
\- Curexa pharmacy ID in **production** environment: 29992 | | effective_date | string | Date from which prescription is effective (time will always be 00:00:00). Format: `Y-m-d` | | order | integer | The prescription ordering | | is_additional_approval_needed | boolean | If the case needs an additional approval | # Question Properties For more information about `questions` and how they affect prescriptions, contact us via Slack or send an email to: [mserota@mdintegrations.com](https://mailto:mserota@mdintegrations.com) We are also available through Slack (partner's channel). | field | type | description | | --- | --- | --- | | question | text\* | Question | | answer | text\* | Answer | | type | string\* | Question type. Available options: `boolean`, `number` or `string` | | important | boolean | Flag to set a question as `important`.

**Important Note**:
This flag is used to identify which questions could affect the final prescription. You could also implement custom logic to set this flag only when specific answers are selected. | | display_in_pdf | boolean | Flag to set if this question will be displayed in the case PDF file | | description | text | A description for your question | | label | string(50) | The question label | | metadata | text | A metadata field | | displayed_options | array | An array of options that were displayed to the patient | ## Service Properties **Important**: a service object can include a **partner_service_id** in order to use predefined information from a partner service. **Note**: Partner services can be found at the partner details page on the Admin panel. Once you know a partner's service id, you can also retrieve its information through the api using the **Get Service** route. | **field** | **type** | | --- | --- | | title | string (**required if partner_service_id is NOT included)** | | description | string (**required if partner_service_id is NOT included)** | | service_type | string (**required if partner_service_id is NOT included)** | | additional_note | string | | partner_service_id | uuid | | clinical_notes | string | | thank_you_note | string | | pharmacy_fax_number | string | | include_drivers_license | boolean | | send_pdf_file_automatically | boolean | | order | integer | # Diseases **Important**: Every disease object will add the disease with the ICD (International Classification of Disease) to the case. **Note**: Diseases can be found in the diseases list on the Admin panel. Once you know a disease's id, you can also retrieve its information through the API using the **Get Disease** route. | field | type | description | | --- | --- | --- | | disease_id | UUID | Disease ID. It can be found at the Admin Panel or through the diseases endpoints. | # Other Params | field | type | description | | --- | --- | --- | | preferred_pharmacy_id | integer | Dosespot Pharmacy ID | | voucher_id | uuid | Optional voucher created by partners. | | questionnaire_id | uuid\* | Quesionnaire ID | | completion_time | string\* | Time in the format "hh:mm:ss" |

item.name: Pharmacies

query.description: National Council for Prescription Drug Programs Pharmacy ID

request.description:
Search DoseSpot pharmacies according to a given query parameter. At least **one** query parameter is required. If no query parameter is provided, an error will be thrown.

This API endpoint forwards the request to DoseSpot in order to search for pharmacies.

Get DoseSpot pharmacy details from a given pharmacy ID.

This API endpoint forwards the request to DoseSpot in order to retrieve the pharmacy details.

**Important note**: 

For each environment (**staging** and **production**) Dosespot works with different IDs for the pharmacies.

For example:

- In a **Staging** environment, the **Curexa** pharmacy ID is **13012**.

- In a **Production** environment, the **Curexa** pharmacy ID changes to **29992**.

Set the patient's preferred pharmacy.

# Payload

|  |  |  |
| --- | --- | --- |
| notify | boolean | Notifies the patient's clinicians about the new pharmacy |

- other
    
- insurance-policy
    
- contract
    
- driver-license
    
- lab-result
    
- photo
    
- av-video
    
- full-body-photo
    
- back-photo
    
- face-photo
    
- avatar-photo
    
- ipledge-document
    
- auth-form

request.description:
Update a patient record on MD Integrations.

# Payload

| field | type | description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| prefix | string(10) | Prefix |
| first_name | string(35) | First name |
| middle_name | string(35) | Middle name |
| last_name | string(35) | Last name |
| ssn | string(11) | Security Social Number. **Format**: `AAAGGSSSS` |
| email | string(80) | E-mail |
| date_of_birth | date | Date of birth, as YYYY-MM-DD |
| driver_license_id | uuid | Patient's Driver License, needs to reference an existing **File**. Created through **Files > Create file**. |
| metadata | string(255) | Field meant to store any information that helps identifying the patient. (e.g., an ID that will be used to identify this patient in another database) |
| gender | integer | Gender (ISO 5218 format) |
| phone_number | string(25) | Phone number - _US format_ |
| phone_type | integer | Phone type. Available options: 2 (cell) or 4 (home) |
| address.address | string(35) | Address |
| address.address2 | string(35) | Additional address - Allows `null` value |
| address.zip_code | string(9) | US zip code - _5 or 9 digit format\*_OR\*CA zip code - 6 alphanumeric characters |
| address.city_id | uuid | City ID. Check **Metadata > States > Cities> Get cities from a given state** to get city ID. If you use this parameter you don't need to use **city_name** parameter. |
| address.city_name | string(35) | City name. If you use this parameter you don't need use **city_id** parameter. |
| address.state_name | string | State name or State USPS abbreviation. If you use this parameter you don't need to use **city_id** parameter. |
| weight | float (from 0 to 999) | Weight in KG \* _required if patient is underage_ |
| height | integer (from 0 to 300) | Height in CM \* _required if patient is underage_ |
| body_temperature | float (from 0.0 to 100.0) | Body temperature in Celsius |
| oxygen_saturation | integer (from 0 to 100) | Oxygen Saturation in percentage |
| special_necessities | text | Special necessities to be informed about in this case |
| allergies | text | Known patient's allergies |
| pregnancy | boolean | Define the patient with a pregnancy |
| current_medications | text | Medications that the patient is currently taking |
| medical_conditions | text | Medical conditions related to the patient. |
| intro_video_id | uuid | Patient's Driver License, needs to reference an existing **File**. Created through **Files > Create file**. |
| is_sms_enabled | boolean | Control the SMS delivery |
| is_email_enabled | boolean | Control the email delivery |
| notify | boolean | Notifies the patient's clinicians about the changes |

Sends the patient data requested to the patient.

# Payload

| field | type | description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| patient_id | uuid | Required if not use the patient email to request their data. |
| patient_email | string | Required if the patient_id is empty. |

query.description: National Council for Prescription Drug Programs Pharmacy ID

request.description:
Search DoseSpot pharmacies according to a given query parameter. At least **one** query parameter is required. If no query parameter is provided, an error will be thrown.

This API endpoint forwards the request to DoseSpot in order to search for pharmacies.

Get DoseSpot pharmacy details from a given pharmacy ID.

This API endpoint forwards the request to DoseSpot in order to retrieve the pharmacy details.

**Important note**: 

For each environment (**staging** and **production**) Dosespot works with different IDs for the pharmacies.

For example:

- In a **Staging** environment, the **Curexa** pharmacy ID is **13012**.

- In a **Production** environment, the **Curexa** pharmacy ID changes to **29992**.
