request.description:
Create a new patient and case. # Case Payload | field | type | description | | --- | --- | --- | | preferred_pharmacy_id | integer | Dosespot Pharmacy ID | | questionnaire_id | uuid\* | Questionnaire ID | | completion_time | string\* | Time in the format "hh:mm:ss" | # Patient Payload | field | type | description | | --- | --- | --- | | prefix | string(10)\* | Prefix | | first_name | string(35)\* | First name | | last_name | string(35)\* | Last name | | ssn | string(11) | Security Social Number. **Format**: `AAAGGSSSS` | | email | string(80)\* | E-mail | | date_of_birth | date\* | Date of birth, as YYYY-MM-DD | | driver_license_id | uuid | Patient's Driver License, needs to reference an existing **File**. Created through **Files > Create file**. | | gender | integer\* | Gender (ISO 5218 format) | | metadata | string(255) | Field meant to store any information that helps identifying the patient. (e.g., an ID that will be used to identify this patient in another database) | | phone_number | string(25)\* | Phone number - _US format_ | | phone_type | integer\* | Phone type. Available options: 2 (cell) or 4 (home) | | address.address | string(35)\* | Address | | address.address2 | string(35) | Additional address - Allows `null` value | | address.zip_code | string(9)\* | US zip code - _5 or 9 digit format\*_OR\*CA zip code - 6 alphanumeric characters | | address.city_name | string(35)\* | City name. | | address.state_name | string\* | State name or State USPS abbreviation. | | weight | float (from 0 to 999) | Weight in KG \* _required if patient is underage_ | | height | integer (from 0 to 300) | Height in CM \* _required if patient is underage_ | | body_temperature | float (from 0.0 to 100.0) | Body temperature in Celsius | | oxygen_saturation | integer (from 0 to 100) | Oxygen Saturation in percentage | | special_necessities | text | Special necessities to be informed about in this case | | allergies | text | Known patient's allergies | | pregnancy | boolean | Define the patient with a pregnancy | | current_medications | text | Medications that the patient is currently taking | | medical_conditions | text | Medical conditions related to the patient. | | intro_video_id | uuid | Patient's Intro Video, needs to reference an existing **File**. Created through **Files > Create file**. | # Case Payload | field | type | description | | --- | --- | --- | | metadata | string(255) | Field meant to store any information that helps identifying the case. (e.g., an ID that will be used to identify this case in another database) | | clinician_id | uuid | Clinician to assign the case to, in case there is a preference for a specific clinician in a specific case. If the field is not sent, a clinician will be assigned according to MDIntegrations algorithm. | | case_prescriptions | array(Prescription) | List of prescriptions to be reviewed by a clinician | | case_files | array(uuid) | Files ID to be attached to the case. The files must be uploaded before creating a case. To upload files check **Partners > Files** | | case_questions | array(Question) | Questions and answers list | | case_services | array(Services) | List of services to be reviewed by a clinician | | tags | array(uuid) | Tags ID to be attached to the case. | ## Prescription Properties **Important**: a prescription object must always contain **exactly one** drug type ID from the following: \[`partner_medication_id`, `partner_compound_id`, `partner_supply_id`\] **Note**: We strongly advise asking our medical team to add medications to your company's dashboard so it's easier to manage prescriptions details from there. Should you need to add a one-time-only medication, you can use the dosespot_medication_id to reference a Dosespot medication directly. | field | type | description | | --- | --- | --- | | partner_compound_id | uuid | Partner compound ID. Check **Partners > Compounds** to get a `partner_compound_id` for a given compound. **Required if** `dosespot_medication_id` and `partner_medication_id` are `null` | | partner_medication_id | uuid | Partner medication ID. It can be found under your company's offerings from the Partners Website. **Required if** `dosespot_medication_id` and `partner_compound_id` are `null` | | partner_supply_id | uuid | Partner supply ID. It can be found under your company's offerings from the Partners Website. **Required if** `dosespot_medication_id` and `partner_compound_id` and `partner_supply_id` are `null` | | refills | integer (from 0 to 99) | Total of refills for the given medication \* Required if `dosespot_medication_id` is present | | quantity | string | Medication quantity string representing a float number (from 0 to 9999999999) **Required if** `dosespot_medication_id` is present | | days_supply | integer (from 0 to 365) | Number of days of supply of the medication provided - Allows `null` value | | directions | string(140) | Instructions about how the medication is administered **Required if** `dosespot_medication_id` is present | | pharmacy_notes | string(210) | Additional information about the prescription | | no_substitutions | boolean | Whether or not a medication or compound can be changed | | dispense_unit_id | integer | \- **Medication** (prescription with a `dosespot_medication_id` included): each medication has one associated dispense unit ID. To get the dispense unit ID associated with a given medication check **Partners > Medications > Get details for a specific medication**;

\- **Compound** (prescription with `partner_compound_id` included): compounds can be associated with any available dispense unit ID. For a list of all available dispense units check **Partners > Dispense Units**. \* Required if `dosespot_medication_id` is present | | pharmacy_id | integer\* | Pharmacy where the prescription must be sent to. Check **Partners > Pharmacies > Get pharmacies** to get a list of all pharmacies available.

**Required if**: `dosespot_medication_id` is present.
**Required if**: `partner_medication_id` or `partner_medication_id` does not have a registered default pharmacy.

**Important note**:

Dosespot works with **different IDs** for pharmacies according to the environment.
Example:
\- Curexa pharmacy ID in **staging** environment: 133012
\- Curexa pharmacy ID in **production** environment: 29992 | | effective_date | string | Date from which prescription is effective (time will always be 00:00:00). Format: `Y-m-d` | | order | integer | The presrcription ordering | # Question Properties For more information about `questions` and how they affect prescriptions, contact us via Slack or send an email to: [mserota@mdintegrations.com](https://mailto:mserota@mdintegrations.com) and [jeff@mdintegrations.com](https://mailto:jeff@mdintegrations.com) We are also available through Slack (partner's channel). | field | type | description | | --- | --- | --- | | question | string(255)\* | Question | | answer | text\* | Answer | | type | string\* | Question type. Available options: `boolean`, `number` or `string` | | important | boolean | Flag to set a question as `important`.

**Important Note**:
This flag is used to identify which questions could affect the final prescription. You could also implement custom logic to set this flag only when specific answers are selected. | | display_in_pdf | boolean | Flag to set if this question will be displayed in the case PDF file | # Diseases **Important**: Every disease object will add the disease with the ICD (International Classification of Disease) to the case. **Note**: Diseses can be found on the diseases page on the Admin panel. Once you know a disease id, you can also retrieve its information through the API using the Get Disease route. | field | type | description | | --- | --- | --- | | disease_id | UUID | Disease ID. It can be found through the diseases endpoints. | ## Service Properties **Important**: a service object can include a **partner_service_id** in order to use predefined information from a partner service. **Note**: Partner services can be found under your company's offerings from the Partners Website. Once you know a partner's service id, you can also retrieve its information through the api using the **Get Service** route. | **field** | **type** | | --- | --- | | title | string (**required if partner_service_id is NOT included)** | | description | string (**required if partner_service_id is NOT included)** | | service_type | string (**required if partner_service_id is NOT included)** | | additional_note | string | | partner_service_id | uuid | | clinical_notes | string | | thank_you_note | string | | pharmacy_fax_number | string | | include_drivers_license | boolean | | send_pdf_file_automatically | boolean | | order | integer |

Check **Partners > Compounds** to get a `partner_compound_id` for a given compound. **Required if** `dosespot_medication_id` and `partner_medication_id` are `null` | | partner_medication_id | uuid | Partner medication ID. It can be found at the Admin Panel. **Required if** `dosespot_medication_id` and `partner_compound_id` are `null` | | partner_supply_id | uuid | Partner supply ID. It can be found at the Admin Panel. **Required if** `dosespot_medication_id` and `partner_compound_id` and `partner_supply_id` are `null` | | refills | integer (from 0 to 99) | Total of refills for the given medication \* Required if `dosespot_medication_id` is present | | quantity | string | Medication quantity string representing a float number (from 0 to 9999999999) **Required if** `dosespot_medication_id` is present | | days_supply | integer (from 0 to 365) | Number of days of supply of the medication provided - Allows `null` value | | directions | string(140) | Instructions about how the medication is administered **Required if** `dosespot_medication_id` is present | | pharmacy_notes | string(210) | Additional information about the prescription | | no_substitutions | boolean | Whether or not a medication or compound can be changed | | dispense_unit_id | integer | \- **Medication** (prescription with a `dosespot_medication_id` included): each medication has one associated dispense unit ID. To get the dispense unit ID associated with a given medication check **Partners > Medications > Get details for a specific medication**;

\- **Compound** (prescription with `partner_compound_id` included): compounds can be associated with any available dispense unit ID. For a list of all available dispense units check **Partners > Dispense Units**. \* Required if `dosespot_medication_id` is present | | pharmacy_id | integer\* | Pharmacy where the prescription must be sent to. Check **Partners > Pharmacies > Get pharmacies** to get a list of all pharmacies available.

**Required if**: `dosespot_medication_id` is present.
**Required if**: `partner_medication_id` or `partner_medication_id` does not have a registered default pharmacy.

**Important note**:

Dosespot works with **different IDs** for pharmacies according to the environment.
Example:
\- Curexa pharmacy ID in **staging** environment: 133012
\- Curexa pharmacy ID in **production** environment: 29992 | | effective_date | string | Date from which prescription is effective (time will always be 00:00:00). Format: `Y-m-d` | | order | integer | The presrcription ordering | # Question Properties For more information about `questions` and how they affect prescriptions, contact us via Slack or send an email to: [mserota@mdintegrations.com](https://mailto:mserota@mdintegrations.com) and [jeff@mdintegrations.com](https://mailto:jeff@mdintegrations.com) We are also available through Slack (partner's channel). | field | type | description | | --- | --- | --- | | question | string(255)\* | Question | | answer | text\* | Answer | | type | string\* | Question type. Available options: `boolean`, `number` or `string` | | important | boolean | Flag to set a question as `important`.

**Important Note**:
This flag is used to identify which questions could affect the final prescription. You could also implement custom logic to set this flag only when specific answers are selected. | | display_in_pdf | boolean | Flag to set if this question will be displayed in the case PDF file | # Diseases **Important**: Every disease object will add the disease with the ICD (International Classification of Disease) to the case. **Note**: Diseases can be found in the diseases list on the Admin panel. Once you know a disease's id, you can also retrieve its information through the API using the **Get Disease** route. | field | type | description | | --- | --- | --- | | disease_id | UUID | Disease ID. It can be found at the Admin Panel or through the diseases endpoints. | ## Service Properties **Important**: a service object can include a **partner_service_id** in order to use predefined information from a partner service. **Note**: Partner services can be found at the partner details page on the Admin panel. Once you know a partner's service id, you can also retrieve its information through the api using the **Get Service** route. | **field** | **type** | | --- | --- | | title | string (**required if partner_service_id is NOT included)** | | description | string (**required if partner_service_id is NOT included)** | | service_type | string (**required if partner_service_id is NOT included)** | | additional_note | string | | partner_service_id | uuid | | clinical_notes | string | | thank_you_note | string | | pharmacy_fax_number | string | | include_drivers_license | boolean | | send_pdf_file_automatically | boolean | | order | integer |

Generate an access token for a clinician.

Considering you have a registered clinician record, you may request an access token using the user's e-mail address and the 2FA code sent to the email.

Access tokens expire **24 hours** after they are issued. Once a token gets expired you will start receiving **401 Unauthorized HTTP** responses. To refresh your access token check _Clinician > Access Tokens > Refresh access token_.

request.description:
Creates a new patient record both on MD Integrations and DoseSpot.

# Payload

| field | type | description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| prefix | string(10)\* | Prefix |
| first_name | string(35)\* | First name |
| last_name | string(35)\* | Last name |
| ssn | string(11) | Security Social Number. **Format**: `AAAGGSSSS` |
| email | string(80)\* | E-mail |
| date_of_birth | date\* | Date of birth, as YYYY-MM-DD |
| driver_license_id | uuid | Patient's Driver License, needs to reference an existing **File**. Created through **Files > Create file**. |
| gender | integer\* | Gender (ISO 5218 format) |
| metadata | string(255) | Field meant to store any information that helps identifying the patient. (e.g., an ID that will be used to identify this patient in another database) |
| phone_number | string(25)\* | Phone number - _US format_ |
| phone_type | integer\* | Phone type. Available options: 2 (cell) or 4 (home) |
| address.address | string(35)\* | Address |
| address.address2 | string(35) | Additional address - Allows `null` value |
| address.zip_code | string(9)\* | Zip code - _5 or 9 digit format_ |
| address.city_id | uuid | City ID. Required if you do not use the **city_name** and **state_name** parameters. Check **Metadata > States > Cities> Get cities from a given state** to get city ID. |
| address.city_name | string(35) | City name. Required if you do not use the **city_id** parameter. Required if you use **state_name** parameter. |
| address.state_name | string | State name or State USPS abbreviation. Required if you do not use the **city_id** parameter. Required if you use **city_name** parameter. |
| weight | float (from 0 to 999) | Weight in KG \* _required if patient is underage_ |
| height | integer (from 0 to 300) | Height in CM \* _required if patient is underage_ |
| body_temperature | float (from 0.0 to 100.0) | Body temperature in Celsius |
| oxygen_saturation | integer (from 0 to 100) | Oxygen Saturation in percentage |
| special_necessities | text | Special necessities to be informed about in this case |
| allergies | text | Known patient's allergies |
| pregnancy | boolean | Define the patient with a pregnancy |
| current_medications | text | Medications that the patient is currently taking |
| medical_conditions | text | Medical conditions related to the patient |
| partner_id\* | uuid | Partner ID. |
| is_live\* | boolean | Define the patient's environment (live or sandbox) |
| is_sms_enabled | boolean | Control the SMS delivery |
| is_email_enabled | boolean | Control the email delivery |

request.description:
Create a new voucher for the questionnaire's flow.

## Payload

**Important**: When creating a voucher you can specify the medications, compounds and services to be used.

| field | type | description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **questionnaire_id** | uuid | Partner Questionnaire ID. It can be found under your company's intake forms from the Partners Website or using **Patient App > Questionnaires > List questionnaires** |
| patient_id | uuid | Patient ID. |
| **hold_status** | boolean | Flag to hold the status to not enter into the MDI flow. It will be **false** by default. |
| demo | boolean | Demo vouchers will not create any patient or cases and will not expire. Default `false` |
| **case_services** | array | It can be empty. |
| **case_services.\*.**partner_service_id | uuid\* | Partner Service ID. It can be found under your company's offerings from the Partners Website. Required if is present. |
| **case_prescriptions** | array | It can be empty. |
| **case_prescriptions.\*.**partner_compound_id | uuid\* | Partner compound ID. Check **Partners > Compounds** to get a `partner_compound_id` for a given compound. Required if is present. |
| **case_prescriptions.\*.**partner_medication_id | uuid\* | Partner medication ID. It can be found under your company's offerings from the Partners Website. Required if is present. |
| **diseases** | array | it can be empty or null. |
| **diseases.\*.**disease_id | UUID | Disease ID. It can be found through the diseases endpoint. Nullable. |
| metadata | string (255) | Metadata value. It can be empty. |

request.description:
Update cases prescriptions. # Payload Receives an array of **Prescriptions**. ## Prescription Properties **Important**: a prescription object must always contain **exactly one** drug type ID from the following: \[`partner_medication_id`, `partner_compound_id`, `partner_supply_id`, `dosespot_supply_ndc`\] **Note**: We strongly advise asking our medical team to add medications to your company's dashboard so it's easier to manage prescriptions details from there. Should you need to add a one-time-only medication, you can use the dosespot_medication_id to reference a Dosespot medication directly. | field | type | description | | --- | --- | --- | | partner_compound_id | uuid | Partner compound ID. Check **Partners > Compounds** to get a `partner_compound_id` for a given compound. **Required if** `dosespot_medication_id` and `partner_supply_id` and `dosespot_supply_ndc` are `null` | | partner_medication_id | uuid | Partner medication ID. It can be found under your company's offerings from the Partners Website. **Required if** `dosespot_medication_id` and `partner_medication_id` and `partner_supply_id` and `dosespot_supply_ndc` are `null` | | partner_supply_id | uuid | Partner supply ID. It can be found under your company's offerings from the Partners Website. **Required if** `dosespot_medication_id` and `partner_compound_id` and `partner_medication_id` and `dosespot_supply_ndc` are `null` | | dosespot_supply_ndc | string | Dosespot Supply NDC code. **Required if** `dosespot_medication_id` and `partner_compound_id` and `partner_medication_id` and `partner_supply_id` are `null` | | refills | integer (from 0 to 99) | Total of refills for the given medication \* Required if `dosespot_medication_id` is present | | quantity | string | Medication quantity string representing a float number (from 0 to 9999999999) **Required if** `dosespot_medication_id` is present | | days_supply | integer (from 0 to 365) | Number of days of supply of the medication provided - Allows `null` value | | directions | string(140) | Instructions about how the medication is administered **Required if** `dosespot_medication_id` is present | | pharmacy_notes | string(210) | Additional information about the prescription | | no_substitutions | boolean | Whether or not a medication or compound can be changed | | dispense_unit_id | integer | \- **Medication** (prescription with a `dosespot_medication_id` included): each medication has one associated dispense unit ID. To get the dispense unit ID associated with a given medication check **Partners > Medications > Get details for a specific medication**;

\- **Compound** (prescription with `partner_compound_id` included): compounds can be associated with any available dispense unit ID. For a list of all available dispense units check **Partners > Dispense Units**. \* Required if `dosespot_medication_id` is present | | pharmacy_id | integer\* | Pharmacy where the prescription must be sent to. Check **Partners > Pharmacies > Get pharmacies** to get a list of all pharmacies available | | effective_date | string | Date from which prescription is effective (time will always be 00:00:00). Format: `Y-m-d` | | order | integer | The prescription ordering | | is_charged_separately | boolean | Allow to charge the precription separateley |

Format: `Y-m-d` | | order | integer | The prescription ordering | | is_charged_separately | boolean | Allow to charge the prescription separately | ### Prescription Status Flow `pending`: When prescription is created on MDI side and not synced with Dosespot yet; `processing`: When MDI receives the Dosespot ID and send through the Dosespot the prescription to Pharmacy; `completed`: When Dosespot responds to the MDI that this prescription has been verified by the pharmacy; `cancelled`: When a doctor cancel the prescription (_when it was synced with Dosespot we try to cancel it there too_)

Set a given case as completed.

**Note:** When this endpoint is called all case prescriptions will be deleted.

A case goes to completed through successfully sending in a prescription or when moved manually from assigned clinician.

In case something goes wrong with the **DoseSpot** integration then the case will be automatically added to the **support** queue.

Set a given case as processing.

Prescriptions will be sent to Dosespot and while there is no confirmation from **DoseSpot**, the case remains in Processing status.

In case something goes wrong with the **DoseSpot** integration then the case will be automatically added to the **support** queue.

Set a given case as Approved status.

It will create all prescriptions on Dosespot but not send them to the pharmacy yet.

| **field** | **type** | **description** |
| --- | --- | --- |
| thank_you_message | string | A thank you message to be sent when the case goes to processing status |
| extra_message | string | An extra message to be sent when the case goes to processing status |

item.description:
The files endpoints from MD Integrations allows you to easily upload, get and delete image files.

If you want to **attach files to cases** or **attach files to messages**, first you will need to upload the file using `POST /partners/files` described in this section. After uploading a file, you will obtain the `file_id` information, which is the required information in order to attach files to cases or messages.

## Attach Files To Cases

Files can be attached to cases through **Clinicians > Cases > Files**.

## Attach Files To Messages

Files can be attached to messages through **Clinicians > Cases > Messages**.

## Response

The request response to upload a file contains the following information:

- `file_id`: corresponding created file ID. This information is used to attach files to cases or messages;
- `name`: file name;
- `url`: URL from original file;
- `url_thumbnail`: URL from thumbnail image with **180x110px**.

request.description:
Create a new case for a given patient. # Payload | field | type | description | | --- | --- | --- | | patient_id | uuid\* | Patient ID. Check **Partners > Cases > Patients** to create new patients | | reference_case_id | uuid | The referenced case UUID | | metadata | string(255) | Field meant to store any information that helps identifying the case. (e.g., an ID that will be used to identify this case in another database) | | clinician_id | uuid | Clinician to assign the case to, in case there is a preference for a specific clinician in a specific case. If the field is not sent, a clinician will be assigned according to MDIntegrations algorithm. | | case_prescriptions | array(Prescription) | List of prescriptions to be reviewed by a clinician | | case_services | array(Service) | List of services to be reviewed by a clinician | | case_files | array(uuid) | Files ID to be attached to the case. The files must be uploaded before creating a case. To upload files check **Partners > Files** | | case_questions | array(Question) | Questions and answers list | | is_chargeable | boolean | Whether or not the case should be charged | | tags | array(object(uuid)) | Tags ID to be attached to the case. | ## Prescription Properties **Important**: a prescription object must always contain **exactly one** drug type ID from the following: \[`partner_medication_id`, `partner_compound_id`, `partner_supply_id`, `dosespot_supply_ndc`\] **Note**: We strongly advise asking our medical team to add medications to your company's dashboard so it's easier to manage prescriptions details from there. Should you need to add a one-time-only medication, you can use the dosespot_medication_id to reference a Dosespot medication directly. | field | type | description | | --- | --- | --- | | partner_compound_id | uuid | Partner compound ID. Check **Partners > Compounds** to get a `partner_compound_id` for a given compound. **Required if** `dosespot_medication_id` and `partner_supply_id` and `dosespot_supply_ndc` are `null` | | partner_medication_id | uuid | Partner medication ID. It can be found under your company's offerings from the Partners Website. **Required if** `dosespot_medication_id` and `partner_medication_id` and `partner_supply_id` and `dosespot_supply_ndc` are `null` | | partner_supply_id | uuid | Partner supply ID. It can be found under your company's offerings from the Partners Website. **Required if** `dosespot_medication_id` and `partner_compound_id` and `partner_medication_id` and `dosespot_supply_ndc` are `null` | | dosespot_supply_ndc | string | Dosespot Supply NDC code. **Required if** `dosespot_medication_id` and `partner_compound_id` and `partner_medication_id` and `partner_supply_id` are `null` | | refills | integer (from 0 to 99) | Total of refills for the given medication \* **Required if** `dosespot_medication_id` or `dosespot_supply_ndc` is present | | quantity | string | Medication quantity string representing a float number (from 0 to 9999999999) **Required if** `dosespot_medication_id` or `dosespot_supply_ndc` is present | | days_supply | integer (from 0 to 365) | Number of days of supply of the medication provided - Allows `null` value | | directions | string(140) | Instructions about how the medication is administered **Required if** `dosespot_medication_id` or `dosespot_supply_ndc` is present | | pharmacy_notes | string(210) | Additional information about the prescription | | no_substitutions | boolean | Whether or not a medication or compound can be changed | | dispense_unit_id | integer | \- **Medication** (prescription with a `dosespot_medication_id` included): each medication has one associated dispense unit ID. To get the dispense unit ID associated with a given medication check **Partners > Medications > Get details for a specific medication**;

\- **Compound** (prescription with `partner_compound_id` included): compounds can be associated with any available dispense unit ID. For a list of all available dispense units check **Partners > Dispense Units**. \* **Required if** `dosespot_medication_id` or `dosespot_supply_ndc` is present | | pharmacy_id | integer | Pharmacy where the prescription must be sent to. Check **Partners > Pharmacies > Get pharmacies** to get a list of all pharmacies available.

**Important note**:

Dosespot works with **different IDs** for pharmacies according to the environment.
Example:
\- Curexa pharmacy ID in **staging** environment: 133012
\- Curexa pharmacy ID in **production** environment: 29992 | | effective_date | string | Date from which prescription is effective (time will always be 00:00:00). Format: `Y-m-d` | | order | integer | The prescriptions ordering | | is_charged_separately | boolean | Allow to charge the prescription separately | ### Prescription Status Flow `pending`: When prescription is created on MDI side and not synced with Dosespot yet; `processing`: When MDI receives the Dosespot ID and send through the Dosespot the prescription to Pharmacy; `completed`: When Dosespot responds to the MDI that this prescription has been verified by the pharmacy; `cancelled`: When a doctor cancel the prescription (_when it was synced with Dosespot we try to cancel it there too_) # Question Properties For more information about `questions` and how they affect prescriptions, contact us via Slack or send an email to: [mserota@mdintegrations.com](https://mailto:mserota@mdintegrations.com) and [jeff@mdintegrations.com](https://mailto:jeff@mdintegrations.com) We are also available through Slack (partner's channel). | field | type | description | | --- | --- | --- | | question | string(255)\* | Question | | answer | text\* | Answer | | type | string\* | Question type. Available options: `boolean`, `number` or `string` | | important | boolean | Flag to set a question as `important`.

**Important Note**:
This flag is used to identify which questions could affect the final prescription. You could also implement custom logic to set this flag only when specific answers are selected. | | display_in_pdf | boolean | Flag to set if this question will be displayed in the case PDF file | | description | text | A description for your question | | label | string(50) | The question label | | metadata | text | A metadata field | | displayed_options | array | An array of options that were displayed to the patient | ## Service Properties **Important**: a service object can include a **partner_service_id** in order to use predefined information from a partner service. **Note**: Partner services can be found under your company's offerings from the Partners Website. Once you know a partner's service id, you can also retrieve its information through the api using the **Get Service** route. | **field** | **type** | | --- | --- | | title | string (**required if partner_service_id is NOT included)** | | description | string (**required if partner_service_id is NOT included)** | | service_type | string (**required if partner_service_id is NOT included)** | | additional_note | string | | partner_service_id | uuid | | clinical_notes | string | | thank_you_note | string | | pharmacy_fax_number | string | | include_drivers_license | boolean | | send_pdf_file_automatically | boolean | | order | integer |

Get the following case details:

- Patient
- Partner
- Priority status and reason
- Status

To get details from `prescriptions`, `files` and `questions` you must use specific API endpoints:

- Files: check **"Clinicians > Cases > Files"**
- Prescriptions: check **"Clinicians > Cases > Prescriptions"**
- Questions: check **"Clinicians > Cases > Questions"**

Return a list of cases according to a given status.

The following statuses are available:

- **Created**: a list of cases create and not assigned yet;
    
- **Assigned**: a list of cases assigned to the clinician waiting for review;
    
- **Waiting**: a list of cases assigned to the clinician, waiting for the patient information or review;
    
- **Cancelled**: a list of cases assigned to the clinician and canceled by the partner;
    
- **Support**: a list of cases assigned to the clinician and transferred to support;
    
- **Processing**: a list of cases assigned to the clinician that were already reviewed and finished by the clinician, but with one or more prescriptions waiting for DoseSpot confirmation;
    
- **Completed**: a list of cases assigned to the clinician with all its prescriptions already confirmed by DoseSpot (prescriptions already verified by the pharmacies).
    

## Payload

| field | type | description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| partners | array(uuid) | If passed, all cases will belong to this partner. |
| clinicians | array(uuid) | If passed, all cases assigned to this clinician will be displayed. |
| sort | string | Sorts by created_at from the Case Status, desc or asc. |
| is_assigned_to_me | boolean | Cases returned will be assigned to the clinician calling the route. |
| tags | array(uuid) | Filter by an array of Tag UUID. |
| diseases | array(uuid) | Filter by an array of Diseases UUID |
| states | array(uuid) | Filter by an array of State UUID |
| case_type | string | Case Type can by New or Follow-up |
| is_sync | boolean | Searchs for Routine or Extended Care encounters. If missing, returns both. |
| is_live | boolean | Defines if the cases from live patients should be returned (defaults to true) |
| is_sandbox | boolean | Defines if the cases from sandbox patients should be returned (defaults to false) |
| is_additional_approval_needed | boolan | Search for cases that needs an additional approvel |

Return a list of cases matching the provided parameters.

In the case where multiple parameters are provided, the response will contain cases that match all the provided parameters, effectively making a logical 'AND'.

All parameters are **optional**. In the case where none is provided, the response will contain all the assigned cases to the clinician. 

## Payload 
| field | type | description |
|-------|:----:|------------:|
|patient_name|string|Search cases by the related patient full name.|
|partners|array of uuid|Search cases by the related Partners. Each item should be a valid partner ID.|
|license_states|array of uuid|Search cases for  the given license states. Each item should be a valid state ID.|
|sort_by_queue_time|'desc' \| 'asc'| Sets the returned cases to be sorted by their queue time in ascending or descending order.|

request.description: Update the currently authenticated clinician record on both MD Integrations and DoseSpot.

Get the following information from the currently authenticated clinician:

- Profile
- Address
- Licenses

In order for a clinician to start prescribing medications or compounds, the clinician must be confirmed by DoseSpot (`dosespot_confirmed`). The confirmation is not instantaneous, so it may take a while.

Get translation for a text.

The Language Code uses [ISO 639-1](https://www.loc.gov/standards/iso639-2/php/code_list.php) two-digit language codes. For a country variant of a language, the table follows the [RFC 5646](https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc5646.html) format of appending a dash followed by an [ISO 3166](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List_of_ISO_3166_country_codes) 2-digit country code. For example, the language code for the Mexican variant of Spanish is `es-MX`.

# Payload

| field | type | description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| source_language | text | Source Language Code |
| target_language | text\* | Target Language Code |
| text | text\* | Text to be translated |

item.description:
# MD Integrations API for Clinicians

## Intro

The MD Integrations API for clinicians is a set of HTTP endpoints that allows clinicians to perform all the required actions in order to review cases for patients on MD Integrations.

## Authentication

MD Integrations for Clinician supports OAuth 2.0 password grant for authorizing API requests. You will need to obtain an access token using an email address and password. Check **Clinician > Access Tokens > Create access token** for more details.


In order to make an authorized request to the API you should use an `Authorization` header with the value Bearer <personal access token>. E.g.:

```http
GET /clinician HTTP/1.1
...
Authorization: Bearer eyJ0eXAiOiJKV1Q...
```

request.description:
Create a new encounter for a given patient. # Payload | field | type | description | | --- | --- | --- | | patient_id | uuid\* | Patient ID. Check **Partners > Cases > Patients** to create new patients | | reference_case_id | uuid | The referenced case UUID | | metadata | string(255) | Field meant to store any information that helps identifying the case. (e.g., an ID that will be used to identify this case in another database) | | clinician_id | uuid\* | Clinician to assign the case to, in case there is a preference for a specific clinician in a specific case. If the field is not sent, a clinician will be assigned according to MDIntegrations algorithm. | | case_prescriptions | array(Prescription) | List of prescriptions to be reviewed by a clinician | | case_services | array(Service) | List of services to be reviewed by a clinician | | case_files | array(uuid) | Files ID to be attached to the case. The files must be uploaded before creating a case. To upload files check **Partners > Files** | | case_questions | array(Question) | Questions and answers list | | is_chargeable | boolean | Whether or not the case should be charged | | tags | array(object(uuid)) | Tags ID to be attached to the case. | ## Prescription Properties **Important**: a prescription object must always contain **exactly one** drug type ID from the following: \[`partner_medication_id`, `partner_compound_id`, `partner_supply_id`, `dosespot_supply_ndc`\] **Note**: We strongly advise asking our medical team to add medications to your company's dashboard so it's easier to manage prescriptions details from there. Should you need to add a one-time-only medication, you can use the dosespot_medication_id to reference a Dosespot medication directly. | field | type | description | | --- | --- | --- | | partner_compound_id | uuid | Partner compound ID. Check **Partners > Compounds** to get a `partner_compound_id` for a given compound. **Required if** `dosespot_medication_id` and `partner_supply_id` and `dosespot_supply_ndc` are `null` | | partner_medication_id | uuid | Partner medication ID. ItIt can be found under your company's offerings from the Partners Website. **Required if** `dosespot_medication_id` and `partner_medication_id` and `partner_supply_id` and `dosespot_supply_ndc` are `null` | | partner_supply_id | uuid | Partner supply ID. It can be found under your company's offerings from the Partners Website. **Required if** `dosespot_medication_id` and `partner_compound_id` and `partner_medication_id` and `dosespot_supply_ndc` are `null` | | dosespot_supply_ndc | string | Dosespot Supply NDC code. **Required if** `dosespot_medication_id` and `partner_compound_id` and `partner_medication_id` and `partner_supply_id` are `null` | | refills | integer (from 0 to 99) | Total of refills for the given medication \* **Required if** `dosespot_medication_id` or `dosespot_supply_ndc` is present | | quantity | string | Medication quantity string representing a float number (from 0 to 9999999999) **Required if** `dosespot_medication_id` or `dosespot_supply_ndc` is present | | days_supply | integer (from 0 to 365) | Number of days of supply of the medication provided - Allows `null` value | | directions | string(140) | Instructions about how the medication is administered **Required if** `dosespot_medication_id` or `dosespot_supply_ndc` is present | | pharmacy_notes | string(210) | Additional information about the prescription | | no_substitutions | boolean | Whether or not a medication or compound can be changed | | dispense_unit_id | integer | \- **Medication** (prescription with a `dosespot_medication_id` included): each medication has one associated dispense unit ID. To get the dispense unit ID associated with a given medication check **Partners > Medications > Get details for a specific medication**;

\- **Compound** (prescription with `partner_compound_id` included): compounds can be associated with any available dispense unit ID. For a list of all available dispense units check **Partners > Dispense Units**. \* **Required if** `dosespot_medication_id` or `dosespot_supply_ndc` is present | | pharmacy_id | integer | Pharmacy where the prescription must be sent to. Check **Partners > Pharmacies > Get pharmacies** to get a list of all pharmacies available.

**Important note**:

Dosespot works with **different IDs** for pharmacies according to the environment.
Example:
\- Curexa pharmacy ID in **staging** environment: 133012
\- Curexa pharmacy ID in **production** environment: 29992 | | effective_date | string | Date from which prescription is effective (time will always be 00:00:00). Format: `Y-m-d` | ### Prescription Status Flow `pending`: When prescription is created on MDI side and not synced with Dosespot yet; `processing`: When MDI receives the Dosespot ID and send through the Dosespot the prescription to Pharmacy; `completed`: When Dosespot responds to the MDI that this prescription has been verified by the pharmacy; `cancelled`: When a doctor cancel the prescription (_when it was synced with Dosespot we try to cancel it there too_) # Question Properties For more information about `questions` and how they affect prescriptions, contact us via Slack or send an email to: [mserota@mdintegrations.com](https://mailto:mserota@mdintegrations.com) and [jeff@mdintegrations.com](https://mailto:jeff@mdintegrations.com) We are also available through Slack (partner's channel). | field | type | description | | --- | --- | --- | | question | string(255)\* | Question | | answer | text\* | Answer | | type | string\* | Question type. Available options: `boolean`, `number` or `string` | | important | boolean | Flag to set a question as `important`.

**Important Note**:
This flag is used to identify which questions could affect the final prescription. You could also implement custom logic to set this flag only when specific answers are selected. | | display_in_pdf | boolean | Flag to set if this question will be displayed in the case PDF file | | description | text | A description for your question | | label | string(50) | The question label | | metadata | text | A metadata field | | displayed_options | array | An array of options that were displayed to the patient | ## Service Properties **Important**: a service object can include a **partner_service_id** in order to use predefined information from a partner service. **Note**: Partner services can be found under your company's offerings from the Partners Website. Once you know a partner's service id, you can also retrieve its information through the api using the **Get Service** route. | **field** | **type** | | --- | --- | | title | string (**required if partner_service_id is NOT included)** | | description | string (**required if partner_service_id is NOT included)** | | service_type | string (**required if partner_service_id is NOT included)** | | additional_note | string | | partner_service_id | uuid | | clinical_notes | string | | thank_you_note | string | | pharmacy_fax_number | string | | include_drivers_license | boolean | | send_pdf_file_automatically | boolean |

Create a new encounter for a given patient.

# Payload

| field | type | description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| status | string\* | The new status (assigned, processing, waiting, cancelled, resubmit, completed) |
| reason | string | The reason. Necessary if the status is cancelled or waiting. |
| clinician_id | uuid\* | Clinician to simulate the action. |

request.description:
Create a encounter prescription. # Payload Receives an object of **Prescription**. ## Prescription Properties **Important**: a prescription object must always contain **exactly one** drug type ID from the following: \[`partner_medication_id`, `partner_compound_id`, `partner_supply_id`, `dosespot_supply_ndc`\] **Note**: We strongly advise asking our medical team to add medications to your company's dashboard so it's easier to manage prescriptions details from there. Should you need to add a one-time-only medication, you can use the dosespot_medication_id to reference a Dosespot medication directly. | field | type | description | | --- | --- | --- | | clinician_id | uuid\* | Clinician to simulate the action | | partner_compound_id | uuid | Partner compound ID. Check **Partners > Compounds** to get a `partner_compound_id` for a given compound. **Required if** `dosespot_medication_id` and `partner_supply_id` and `dosespot_supply_ndc` are `null` | | partner_medication_id | uuid | Partner medication ID. It can be found under your company's offerings from the Partners Website. **Required if** `dosespot_medication_id` and `partner_medication_id` and `partner_supply_id` and `dosespot_supply_ndc` are `null` | | partner_supply_id | uuid | Partner supply ID. It can be found under your company's offerings from the Partners Website. **Required if** `dosespot_medication_id` and `partner_compound_id` and `partner_medication_id` and `dosespot_supply_ndc` are `null` | | dosespot_supply_ndc | string | Dosespot Supply NDC code. **Required if** `dosespot_medication_id` and `partner_compound_id` and `partner_medication_id` and `partner_supply_id` are `null` | | refills | integer (from 0 to 99) | Total of refills for the given medication \* Required if `dosespot_medication_id` is present | | quantity | string | Medication quantity string representing a float number (from 0 to 9999999999) **Required if** `dosespot_medication_id` is present | | days_supply | integer (from 0 to 365) | Number of days of supply of the medication provided - Allows `null` value | | directions | string(140) | Instructions about how the medication is administered **Required if** `dosespot_medication_id` is present | | pharmacy_notes | string(210) | Additional information about the prescription | | no_substitutions | boolean | Whether or not a medication or compound can be changed | | dispense_unit_id | integer | \- **Medication** (prescription with a `dosespot_medication_id` included): each medication has one associated dispense unit ID. To get the dispense unit ID associated with a given medication check **Partners > Medications > Get details for a specific medication**;

\- **Compound** (prescription with `partner_compound_id` included): compounds can be associated with any available dispense unit ID. For a list of all available dispense units check **Partners > Dispense Units**. \* Required if `dosespot_medication_id` is present | | pharmacy_id | integer\* | Pharmacy where the prescription must be sent to. Check **Partners > Pharmacies > Get pharmacies** to get a list of all pharmacies available | | effective_date | string | Date from which prescription is effective (time will always be 00:00:00). Format: `Y-m-d` | ### Prescription Status Flow `pending`: When prescription is created on MDI side and not synced with Dosespot yet; `processing`: When MDI receives the Dosespot ID and send through the Dosespot the prescription to Pharmacy; `completed`: When Dosespot responds to the MDI that this prescription has been verified by the pharmacy; `cancelled`: When a doctor cancel the prescription (_when it was synced with Dosespot we try to cancel it there too_)

request.description:
Create a new patient and encounter. # Case Payload | field | type | description | | --- | --- | --- | | preferred_pharmacy_id | integer | Dosespot Pharmacy ID | | questionnaire_id | uuid\* | Questionnaire ID | | completion_time | string\* | Time in the format "hh:mm:ss" | # Patient Payload | field | type | description | | --- | --- | --- | | prefix | string(10)\* | Prefix | | first_name | string(35)\* | First name | | last_name | string(35)\* | Last name | | ssn | string(11) | Security Social Number. **Format**: `AAAGGSSSS` | | email | string(80)\* | E-mail | | date_of_birth | date\* | Date of birth, as YYYY-MM-DD | | driver_license_id | uuid | Patient's Driver License, needs to reference an existing **File**. Created through **Files > Create file**. | | gender | integer\* | Gender (ISO 5218 format) | | metadata | string(255) | Field meant to store any information that helps identifying the patient. (e.g., an ID that will be used to identify this patient in another database) | | phone_number | string(25)\* | Phone number - _US format_ | | phone_type | integer\* | Phone type. Available options: 2 (cell) or 4 (home) | | address.address | string(35)\* | Address | | address.address2 | string(35) | Additional address - Allows `null` value | | address.zip_code | string(9)\* | US zip code - _5 or 9 digit format\*_OR\*CA zip code - 6 alphanumeric characters | | address.city_name | string(35)\* | City name. | | address.state_name | string\* | State name or State USPS abbreviation. | | weight | float (from 0 to 999) | Weight in KG \* _required if patient is underage_ | | height | integer (from 0 to 300) | Height in CM \* _required if patient is underage_ | | allergies | text | Known patient's allergies | | pregnancy | boolean | Define the patient with a pregnancy | | current_medications | text | Medications that the patient is currently taking | | intro_video_id | uuid | Patient's Intro Video, needs to reference an existing **File**. Created through **Files > Create file**. | # Case Payload | field | type | description | | --- | --- | --- | | metadata | string(255) | Field meant to store any information that helps identifying the case. (e.g., an ID that will be used to identify this case in another database) | | clinician_id | uuid | Clinician to assign the case to, in case there is a preference for a specific clinician in a specific case. If the field is not sent, a clinician will be assigned according to MDIntegrations algorithm. | | case_prescriptions | array(Prescription) | List of prescriptions to be reviewed by a clinician | | case_files | array(uuid) | Files ID to be attached to the case. The files must be uploaded before creating a case. To upload files check **Partners > Files** | | case_questions | array(Question) | Questions and answers list | | case_services | array(Services) | List of services to be reviewed by a clinician | | tags | array(uuid) | Tags ID to be attached to the case. | ## Prescription Properties **Important**: a prescription object must always contain **exactly one** drug type ID from the following: \[`partner_medication_id`, `partner_compound_id`, `partner_supply_id`\] **Note**: We strongly advise to add a medication to the Admin Panel so it's easier to manage a prescription's details from there. Should you need to add a one-time-only medication, you can use the dosespot_medication_id to reference a Dosespot medication directly. | field | type | description | | --- | --- | --- | | partner_compound_id | uuid | Partner compound ID. Check **Partners > Compounds** to get a `partner_compound_id` for a given compound. **Required if** `dosespot_medication_id` and `partner_medication_id` are `null` | | partner_medication_id | uuid | Partner medication ID. It can be found at the Admin Panel. **Required if** `dosespot_medication_id` and `partner_compound_id` are `null` | | partner_supply_id | uuid | Partner supply ID. It can be found at the Admin Panel. **Required if** `dosespot_medication_id` and `partner_compound_id` and `partner_supply_id` are `null` | | refills | integer (from 0 to 99) | Total of refills for the given medication \* Required if `dosespot_medication_id` is present | | quantity | string | Medication quantity string representing a float number (from 0 to 9999999999) **Required if** `dosespot_medication_id` is present | | days_supply | integer (from 0 to 365) | Number of days of supply of the medication provided - Allows `null` value | | directions | string(140) | Instructions about how the medication is administered **Required if** `dosespot_medication_id` is present | | pharmacy_notes | string(210) | Additional information about the prescription | | no_substitutions | boolean | Whether or not a medication or compound can be changed | | dispense_unit_id | integer | \- **Medication** (prescription with a `dosespot_medication_id` included): each medication has one associated dispense unit ID. To get the dispense unit ID associated with a given medication check **Partners > Medications > Get details for a specific medication**;

\- **Compound** (prescription with `partner_compound_id` included): compounds can be associated with any available dispense unit ID. For a list of all available dispense units check **Partners > Dispense Units**. \* Required if `dosespot_medication_id` is present | | pharmacy_id | integer\* | Pharmacy where the prescription must be sent to. Check **Partners > Pharmacies > Get pharmacies** to get a list of all pharmacies available.

**Required if**: `dosespot_medication_id` is present.
**Required if**: `partner_medication_id` or `partner_medication_id` does not have a registered default pharmacy.

**Important note**:

Dosespot works with **different IDs** for pharmacies according to the environment.
Example:
\- Curexa pharmacy ID in **staging** environment: 133012
\- Curexa pharmacy ID in **production** environment: 29992 | | effective_date | string | Date from which prescription is effective (time will always be 00:00:00). Format: `Y-m-d` | # Question Properties For more information about `questions` and how they affect prescriptions, contact us via Slack or send an email to: [mserota@mdintegrations.com](https://mailto:mserota@mdintegrations.com) and [jeff@mdintegrations.com](https://mailto:jeff@mdintegrations.com) We are also available through Slack (partner's channel). | field | type | description | | --- | --- | --- | | question | string(255)\* | Question | | answer | text\* | Answer | | type | string\* | Question type. Available options: `boolean`, `number` or `string` | | important | boolean | Flag to set a question as `important`.

**Important Note**:
This flag is used to identify which questions could affect the final prescription. You could also implement custom logic to set this flag only when specific answers are selected. | | display_in_pdf | boolean | Flag to set if this question will be displayed in the case PDF file | # Diseases **Important**: Every disease object will add the disease with the ICD (International Classification of Disease) to the case. **Note**: Diseases can be found in the diseases list on the Admin panel. Once you know a disease's id, you can also retrieve its information through the API using the **Get Disease** route. | field | type | description | | --- | --- | --- | | disease_id | UUID | Disease ID. It can be found at the Admin Panel or through the diseases endpoints. | ## Service Properties **Important**: a service object can include a **partner_service_id** in order to use predefined information from a partner service. **Note**: Partner services can be found at the partner details page on the Admin panel. Once you know a partner's service id, you can also retrieve its information through the api using the **Get Service** route. | **field** | **type** | | --- | --- | | title | string (**required if partner_service_id is NOT included)** | | description | string (**required if partner_service_id is NOT included)** | | service_type | string (**required if partner_service_id is NOT included)** | | additional_note | string | | partner_service_id | uuid | | clinical_notes | string | | thank_you_note | string | | pharmacy_fax_number | string | | include_drivers_license | boolean | | send_pdf_file_automatically | boolean |

request.description: Get processing cases queue.
Get servers load.
Get servers memory.
Get servers storage.

With this endpoint, you can exchange your **Client ID** and **Client Secret** for an access token with _limited lifetime_.

The access token allows your application to make authenticated requests to the MD Integrations API.

For more details on the expected flow, you can checkout the **Authentication** section in the **Introduction**.

## Payload

For this request, all fields in the payload are **required**.

| field | type | description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| grant_type | string\* | Should always be `client_credentials` |
| client_id | string\* | Client ID credential |
| client_secret | string\* | Client Secret credential |
| scope | string\* | Should always be `\*` |

## Expired tokens

Whenever an access token expires, you should request a brand new one for making new requests.

## Client Credentials

Your credentials will be provided to you by the MD Integrations team.

Keep in mind the importance of **protecting your credentials** to avoid unauthorized access.

request.description:
Creates a partner, attaches its categories, creates a panel user, and attaches it to the partner.

# Payload

| **field** | **type** |
| --- | --- |
| partner\* | object |
| categories | array |
| panel_user\* | object |

# Payload (partner)

| field | **type** |
| --- | --- |
| name\* | string |
| notification_priority | string |
| active | boolean |
| logo_file_id | uuid |
| operation_country_id\* | uuid |
| address_id | uuid |
| customer_support_email | string |
| operations_support_email | string |
| patient_message_capability | string |
| support_message_capability | string |
| messaging_app_primary_color | string |
| support_hours | string |
| support_location | string (international, us-based) |
| use_intake_form | boolean |
| use_messaging | boolean |
| messaging_app_secondary_color | string |
| messaging_app_background_color | string |
| partner_notes | string |
| thank_you_note_header | string |
| thank_you_note_footer | string |
| vouched_integration_type | string |
| text_message_integration_type | string (disabled, automatic) |
| enable_av_flow | boolean |
| accepted_at | datetime |
| parent_company | string |
| recaptcha\* | string |
| is_statistics_enabled | boolean |
| force_is_additional_approval_need | boolean |
| is_patient_allowed_choose_notification_inbox | boolean |

# Payload (categories)

| **field** | **type** |
| --- | --- |
| id\* | uuid |

# Payload (panel_user)

| **field** | **type** |
| --- | --- |
| name\* | string |
| email\* | string |
| timezone_id | uuid |

request.description:
This endpoint is designed to show how events will be dispatched.

#### Request Body Parameters

The request body will be in JSON format and include the following parameters:

- **entity_type** (string): Specifies the type of entity that is being updated. Possible values include `partner`, `panel_user`, `support_staff`, `contact` or `file`
    
- **event_type** (string): Indicates the type of event being reported.  
    Valid options are `created` , `updated`, `deleted`, `detached`, `file_attached`.
    
- **fyle_type** (string): Only used when the entity type is `file` . Valid options are `contract` or `insurance`.
    
- **mdi_record_id** (string): A unique identifier for the record in the MDI system.
    
- **mdi_partner_id** (string): A unique identifier for the partner in the MDI system.
    
- **email** (string): The email address associated with the entity (if exists)
    
- **changes** (object): An object that outlines the changes made. It contains:
    
    - (object): An object with `previous` and `current` values representing the first name change. It can be any field.
        
- **change_source** (string): Indicates the source of the change. Possible values include `mdi` or `hubspot`.
